            I’ll treat this as a hostile technical referee report: isolate the core claim chain, separate
            proved/imported/open material, and attack definitions, normalizations, hidden
            assumptions, and overclaims.
            The main vulnerability is sharper now: the determinant/K₀ package may be clean but
            tautological, while the physics language risks upgrading a recognition ledger into
            realization.
            Thought for 11m 9s
            I’m going to be brutal. The draft is strongest as a normalization-and-separation paper:
            it carefully distinguishes the Borcherds product, the Gritsenko–Nikulin denominator
            algebra, the OP/DT scalar square, and an open compact Hall/factorization realization. It
            is weakest whenever the prose makes the reader feel that these layers are closer than
            they are. The main danger is not that the automorphic mathematics is fake; the main
            danger is that the physical and Hall-theoretic interpretation is not yet mathematically
            supported by an additive charge category. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            The paper’s core theorem is basically:
                                                                        Borcherds/Gritsenko–Nikulin
                                                      ϕ0,1      ​                                                 ​       Δ5   ​




            with a careful convention that the theta-normalized form has leading coefficient 64, so
            that the monic product is D5 = 64−1Δ5. That part is fine and externally anchored:
            Gritsenko–Nikulin explicitly write 64−1Δ5 as the product with exponents f (nm, l), the
                                                                    ​                ​




            Fourier coefficients of ϕ0,1. arXiv +1
                                                                                         ​




                                                        ​




            But the paper risks sounding like it has built a BPS square root for K3 × E . It has not.
            It has built a virtual K0-determinant and aligned it with an existing BKM denominator
            theorem. Your own draft repeatedly admits this: the half-index object is only a
                                                  ​




            K0 (sVect) representative with no canonical Hilbert-space half, no parity lift, and no
            operator product; the compact Hall/chiral realization is explicitly open.
                    ​




                        raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            The single most serious foundational problem is this:
                                                                                (n, l, m)

            is being used in three incompatible ways: Fourier exponent, Lorentzian BKM root
            degree, and would-be physical/Hall charge degree. The first two are additive. The third
            is generally not additive if (n, l, m) are Gram invariants of physical charges.
            That has to be fixed before any compact Hall/factorization story can be more than a
            recognition wish-list.

            1. Fatal issue: Gram invariants are not physical Hall charges
            You define
                                                                         Γgram = Γind = Z3
                                                                                 ​                ​




            and explicitly say it is “notation for Gram invariants and Fourier indices,” not a
            microscopic charge lattice. Then later the compact Hall discussion assumes an
            extension-closed Hall category with numerical Gram-index lattice Γind , semistable
            stacks indexed by γ ∈ Γind , and Hall correspondences landing in γ + η.
                                                                                                                                   ​




                                                            ​




                        raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…                raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                           Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            That is not a small technical gap. It is structurally wrong unless you supply a new
            construction.
            If physical charges are electric-magnetic pairs (Q, P ), then
                                                          Q2                                                                          P2
                                               n=            ,                     l = Q ⋅ P,                        m=                  .
                                                          2                                                                           2
                                                                  ​                                                                               ​




            But for two charge pairs,
                                                  (Q1 + Q2 )2   Q21   Q22
                                                              =     +     + Q1 ⋅ Q2 ,
                                                          ​                ​                ​               ​




                                                      2         2     2
                                                                               ​                ​               ​                 ​           ​




                             (Q1 + Q2 ) ⋅ (P1 + P2 ) = Q1 ⋅ P1 + Q2 ⋅ P2 + Q1 ⋅ P2 + Q2 ⋅ P1 ,
                                 ​         ​          ​               ​                 ​           ​   ​             ​                   ​           ​   ​   ​




                                                    (P1 + P2 )2   P12   P22
                                                                =     +     + P1 ⋅ P2 .
                                                              ​            ​                ​               ​




                                                        2          2     2
                                                                               ​                ​               ​             ​           ​




            So the map
                                                                          (Q, P ) ↦ (n, l, m)

            is quadratic. It does not send physical charge addition to addition in Z3, except in
            special cross-orthogonal situations. A Hall algebra must be graded by an additive K -
            theory, Mukai, or electromagnetic charge lattice. It cannot be fundamentally graded by
            the Gram invariants unless you prove a quotient/descent theorem saying that the Hall
            product descends through this nonlinear invariant map.
            The same issue appears on the D6–D2–D0 side. You define rank-one charges
                                         γ(h, d, n) = (1; (βh , d); n),             ​
                                                                                                                    βh = s1 + hF ,
                                                                                                                          ​           ​




            and the scalar DT series sums over h, d, n. But the rank-one D6 sector is not extension-
            closed under Hall multiplication: extensions/direct sums of rank-one objects naturally
            leave the rank-one sector. Also h − 1 = β 2/2 is quadratic in β , not additive. The family
            βh = s1 + hF is not closed under addition in the way a Hall grading would require.
                ​        ​

            What a hostile referee will say
            “You have a beautiful additive root lattice for the automorphic product, but you have not
            shown that it is the additive charge lattice of the compact BPS category. You have
            shown an additive algebra of Fourier exponents, not an additive algebra of physical
            charges.”

            Required repair
            Introduce two lattices, not one:
                                                                  Γphys   ​               and               Γgram .  ​




            The Hall category must be graded by Γphys. Then define a non-additive invariant map
                                                                                           ​




                                                                   π : Γphys → Γgram .         ​                 ​




            The automorphic product is the π-pushed-forward index. If you want a primitive Lie
            algebra graded by Γgram , you must prove a descent theorem:

            compatible with brackets. Without this, the compact Hall/factorization target cannot
            literally be gΔ -graded by (n, l, m).

            2. The “half K3 index” is not physical data
            The draft says:
                                               ZK3 = 2ϕ0,1 ,
                                                         ​          ​                     ϕ0,1 = ∑ f (n, l)q n rl .
                                                                                                       ​




            Then it introduces
                                             Un,l ∈ K0 (sVectfd ),
                                                 ​           ​                ​
                                                                                                           sdim Un,l = f (n, l),
                                                                                                                             ​




            and correctly says this is not a canonical half of the K3 BPS Hilbert space.
                    raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            That is honest, but it also means the phrase “one-particle index” is dangerous. A hostile
            physicist will object:
                                                                       BPS
                                                                 sdim Hn,l = 2f (n, l)             ​




            is an index of the K3 sigma model. The half-index f (n, l) is an arithmetic input to the
            Borcherds lift. It is not a protected subspace, not a superselection sector, and not a
            Hilbert-space trace.
            The situation is even sharper for negative coefficients. If f (n, l) < 0, your model takes
            an odd vector space of dimension −f (n, l). That is a perfectly valid K0-representative.                               ​

            4. The OP/DT scalar square is a scalar theorem, not evidence for the
            square root
            The OP/DT part is externally legitimate: Oberdieck–Pixton prove the primitive K3 × E
            Igusa cusp form conjecture in the reduced Gromov–Witten setting, and Oberdieck’s
            reduced stable-pair work compares quotient Behrend-weighted definitions and reduced
            virtual-class definitions. arXiv +1
            But the physics/mathematics relation is easy to overstate. Bryan’s account makes the
            scalar conjecture
                                                                                  1
                                                             DT (X) = −                    ​




                                                                                 χ10   ​




            with the monic product convention for χ10 , and also emphasizes that ordinary DT
            invariants vanish because of the E -action, forcing a reduced quotient theory.
                                                                     ​




              UBC Mathematics

                                              2      −1   2
                                       χOP

                                              ​          ​       ​
                                                                                    X

            normalization. The number 4096 is not a degeneracy, not an anomaly, not an orientation
            character, and not a square-root datum. You say this, but the scalar-square section is
            still positioned so prominently that readers may infer more.

            6. The E3 -factorization language is underdefined
                                   ​




            You repeatedly refer to a “holomorphic E3-factorization algebra” on the Calabi–Yau
            threefold K3 × E . You also say the symbol is a placeholder and not a constructed
                                                                        ​




            functor. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…
            That is good honesty, but the phrase itself is not standard enough to carry the
            argument. In a real six-dimensional topological theory one expects E6-type local
            structure. In a holomorphic theory on a complex threefold one gets a holomorphic
                                                                                                                         ​




            factorization algebra, but not automatically an ordinary little-disks E3-algebra unless a
            precise holomorphic/formality/framing reduction is supplied. You list “formality point”
                                                                                                                     ​




            and “S 3-framing witness,” but this is currently a slogan, not a definition.
            Hostile objection
            “What exactly is an E3-algebra on a complex threefold here? Is this holomorphic disks,
            real little 3-disks, Costello–Gwilliam factorization, E3 from complex dimension three, or
                                                ​




            a framed E3 after integrating over K3? Which operad acts, and on what category?”
                                                                                                        ​




                               ​




            Required repair
            Define the exact structure before using the symbol:
                                                             AX ∈ AlgO (Facthol (X))
                                                                ​           ​                       ​




            for some named operad or factorization category. If “E3” is only heuristic, remove it
            from theorem-level statements and say “holomorphic factorization algebra on X ” until
                                                                                                            ​




            the operadic structure is specified.

            The attack is simple: all compact physics is hidden inside the undefined functor.

            8. The Ran(E) obstruction is good, but phrase it narrowly
            Your observation is correct: if a one-dimensional subscheme C ⊂ K3 × E has
            positive elliptic degree, then pE (C) = E . Therefore such a curve cannot be localized at
            finitely many points of E . The manuscript uses this to argue that naive support-local
                                                             ​




            factorization on Ran(E) sees only projection-finite sectors.
                    raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            That is one of the strongest original conceptual points in the draft.
            But do not overstate it as a general obstruction to locality. It is an obstruction to a
            particular pushforward-to-Ran(E) model. A six-dimensional local theory on K3 × E
            can still have local observables; the problem is that positive elliptic-degree curve
            sectors are global from the E -projection viewpoint.
            Required repair
            Replace “locality obstruction” with:
               projection-to-E support-locality obstruction.
            That avoids a physicist objecting that you are confusing spacetime locality with support-
            locality after dimensional reduction.

            9. The BKM denominator is not a microscopic BPS algebra
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                               Attack Whitepaper Analysis




            The denominator algebra is real mathematics: Gritsenko–Nikulin construct a generalized
            Lorentzian Kac–Moody superalgebra whose denominator is the normalized Δ5 form.
            Their paper explicitly gives Δ5 as the correcting automorphic form for the rank-three
                                                                                                                                 ​




            Cartan matrix with entries 2 on the diagonal and −2 off the diagonal. arXiv
                                                             ​




            But the BKM algebra is not automatically the algebra of BPS states. You already know
            this and state it. The determinant only fixes signed supermultiplicities:
                                                     smult(α) = dim gα,0 − dim gα,1 .  ​                     ​




            It does not fix the two dimensions separately, invisible zero-superdimension root
            spaces, or brackets. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…
            A very simple adversarial counterexample is already implicit in your paper:
            Take any graded super vector space B with
                                                                 sdim Bn,l,m = f (nm, l)
                                                                            ​




            and give it the zero bracket. It has the same determinant, but it is not gΔ . Your own
            Proposition 10.31 says the determinant forgets brackets, parity splits, simple-root
                                                                                                                     5
                                                                                                                             ​


                                                                                                                         ​




            representatives, relation constants, and Hopf-pairing radicals.
                     raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            Required repair
            Every time you write
                                                        smult gΔ5 ,α(n,l,m) = f (nm, l),
                                                                       ​
                                                                                   ​




            immediately distinguish:
                    signed root Euler characteristic =
                                                      state space =
                                                                    primitive Hall bracket.

            You do this in places. Do it everywhere.

            10. The physical duality group is underspecified
            The paper uses Sp4(Z) as the automorphic group of Δ5 and then discusses physical
            charge invariants. That is fine automorphically, but physically K3 × E type II
                                            ​                                                       ​




            compactification has a much larger charge/duality structure. Sp4(Z) acts naturally on                ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                           10/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                       Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            genus-two chemical potentials and the triple of T-duality invariants, not on the full
            microscopic charge lattice.
            So the paper needs to say:
                                                                  Sp4 (Z)
                                                                        ​




            is the modular group of the genus-two counting function / invariant chemical potential,
            not the full physical U-duality group of the compactification.
            Otherwise a physicist will object that the true BPS charge lattice and wall-crossing data
            contain more information than (Q2/2, Q ⋅ P , P 2/2). Multi-centered wall crossing, for
            example, depends on splittings and symplectic pairings of constituents, not only the
            final Gram matrix.

              The Sp4(Z) action here is the arithmetic symmetry of the genus-two automorphic

            11. The m = 0 boundary factors are not one-particle K3 states
            You define
                                                              Vn,l,m = Unm,l .
                                                                    ​              ​




            For m > 0, this matches the Borcherds/DMVV-style exponent f (nm, l). But for m = 0
            , you get infinitely many boundary factors with the same K3 coefficient f (0, l) as n
            varies. Those are cusp/Weyl/oscillator boundary factors in the product. They are not
            literal copies of a K3 BPS Hilbert space indexed by arbitrary n.
            You note that m = 0 are cusp-boundary terms, but the “one-particle object” language
            still invites a wrong physical reading.
            Required repair
            Split the virtual object:
                                                             V = Vbulk ⊕ Vcusp .
                                                                            ​          ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                               11/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                     Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            Then state:
               Vbulk is the Borcherds/Hecke exponent package for m > 0;
               Vcusp is a boundary/Weyl correction package;
                           ​




               neither is a microscopic one-particle Hilbert space.

            12. Boundary rows: good ledger, but too much row-map danger
            The Cléry–Gritsenko result really is an eight-row classification of genus-two Siegel
            modular forms with the simplest diagonal divisor. arXiv
            Govindarajan’s CHL paper also states that the square roots of the relevant twisted dyon
            Siegel forms are precisely the dd-modular forms classified by Cléry–Gritsenko and arise
            as Weyl–Kac–Borcherds denominators. arXiv
            So your ledger is defensible. But this part is high-risk because you are mixing:
                Cléry–Gritsenko row order,
                your row order,
                CHL GN (M ) notation,
                product-degree lattices,
                                   ​




                multiplier covers,
                scalar squares,
                denominator identities,
                physical hosts.
            A hostile reader will search for one row-map mismatch and use it to discredit the
            appendix.
            Required repair
            Move the eight-row discussion to a separate paper or a clearly optional appendix. If
            kept, every row needs a compact table with:
                                           (Fj , Γj , cover, νj , seed, ρj , Lj , source theorem).
                                              ​     ​         ​          ​     ​




            For F7 = ∇3/2, do not merely say “μ4-cover.” Explicitly state which multiplier is pulled
            back, what becomes single-valued, and which square-root branch is used.
                      ​                ​                          ​

            I would restructure the manuscript around a cleaner thesis:
            Theorem A: normalization
                              Dϕ0,1 (Z) = 64q 1/2 r1/2 s1/2 ∏(1 − q n rl sm )f (nm,l) = Δ5 (Z).
                                       ​
                                           ​                                             ​                                        ​

            Open Problem D: compact realization
            Construct a real additive physical charge lattice, Hall category, orientation, protected
            integration, chiral specialization, and primitive bracket whose invariant pushforward
            recovers the BKM denominator.
            This framing would be almost unassailable.

            is already a physical Hall charge. It is not. It is a Fourier/root degree. The physical charge
            lattice problem is the central unresolved issue.
            Your best defense is to lean into that: make the paper a precise no-overclaim ledger.
            The title could remain provocative, but the theorem statements should be austere. The
            compact K3 × E realization should be framed as a recognition problem constrained by
            the automorphic data, not as something the determinant has substantially built.

            1. The physical charge lattice
            Let S be the K3 surface and X = S × E . Put
                                    ΛS := H (S, Z) = H 0 (S, Z) ⊕ H 2 (S, Z) ⊕ H 4 (S, Z)

            with Mukai pairing
                                             (r, D, s) ⋅ (r′ , D′ , s′ ) = D ⋅ D′ − rs′ − r′ s.

            This is the K3 Mukai lattice. It is even, unimodular, and has signature (4, 20). In the full
            N = 4 charge theory one may enlarge this to the Narain lattice Γ6,22 , but for the
            algebraic D-brane sector of K3 × E , H (S, Z) is the correct geometric starting point.

            5. The Gram-corrected charge extension
            We can package the defect as an honest additive object.
            Define
                                                                      ΓX := Γphys
                                                                             X ​  ⊕ Γind .                        ​               ​




            For c, c′ ∈ Γphys
                         X    and T , T ′ ∈ Γind , define
                                      ​                                    ​




                                          (c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ ) = (c + c′ , T + T ′ + B(c, c′ )).

            Because B is symmetric bilinear, this product is commutative and associative. Indeed,
                                                  B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c, c′′ ) + B(c′ , c′′ ),

            so
            ((c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ )) ⋆ (c′′ , T ′′ ) = (c + c′ + c′′ , T + T ′ + T ′′ + B(c, c′ ) + B(c, c′′ ) + B(c′ ,

            and the same expression is obtained from
                                                         (c, T ) ⋆ ((c′ , T ′ ) ⋆ (c′′ , T ′′ )).

            The identity is (0, 0). The inverse of (c, T ) is
                                                                 (−c, −T + B(c, c)).

            Now define
                                                                  s : Γphys
                                                                       X    ⟶ ΓX

            by

            Therefore
                                            s : Γphys

            This is the precise mathematical repair of the non-additivity problem.
            The physical charge lattice remains Γphys
                                                   X . The Gram-index lattice becomes a quadratic
            invariant, and the obstruction to additivity is the explicit bilinear cocycle B . The central

            extension ΓX is the minimal bookkeeping object that makes the Gram data functorial

30/04/2026, 01:14                                                             Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            Let
                                                              c = (Q, P ) ∈ Λ ⊕ Λ.

            Define the rank-two charge sublattice
                                                             Mc := ZQ + ZP ⊂ Λ.
                                                                ​




            The Gram form on Mc is             ​




                                                               Q2           Q⋅P
                                                   Gc = (                       ) = 2T (Π(c)).
                                                              Q⋅P            P2
                                                      ​                 ​          ​




            The pair c = (Q, P ) is primitive saturated, or torsion-one, if
                                                              Mc = Λ ∩ (Mc ⊗ Q).
                                                                    ​                  ​




            Equivalently, in a coordinate basis this is the condition usually written
                                                                gcd(Q ∧ P ) = 1.

            The original DVV Igusa-counting formula is the torsion-one dyon formula; torsion > 1
            dyons require extra discrete data beyond Q2, P 2, Q ⋅ P . arXiv +1
            Now use the lattice theorem.
            For an even unimodular indefinite lattice Λ with enough hyperbolic planes, primitive
            embeddings of a fixed even rank-two lattice M are unique up to O(Λ) once the
            complement has sufficiently large rank relative to the discriminant length. The standard
            Nikulin criterion gives uniqueness when the positive and negative signatures have room
            and
                                                          rk(Λ) − rk(M ) ≥ 2 + ℓ(AM ),               ​




            where AM = M ∨ /M is the discriminant group. For rank-two M , ℓ(AM ) ≤ 2, while
            H (S , Z) has rank 24 and Γ6,22 has rank 28, so the condition is overwhelmingly
                             ​                                                                             ​




            satisfied away from the usual boundary degeneracies. math.brown.edu
            Thus, for primitive saturated pairs,
                           O(Λ)\{(Q, P ) ∈ Λ2 : Π(Q, P ) = (n, l, m), gcd(Q ∧ P ) = 1}                         ​




            is a single orbit, subject to the standard signature and chamber conditions.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                         23/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                                   Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            Therefore the protected index in the primitive torsion-one sector descends:
                                           Ω(Q, P ) = Ω(n, l, m),                                                                     (n, l, m) = Π(Q, P ).

            This is the mathematical meaning of the Igusa variables:
                                                                                                                                                                                                 phys
               Γind = Sym2 (Z2 )half is the primitive T-duality-invariant quotient of ΓX .
                      ​                                    ​                                                                                                                                            ​   ​




            For torsion > 1, this is false unless one augments Γind by the torsion invariant.                                                          ​

            8. What this proves for the whitepaper
            The physical charge lattice problem can now be stated as a theorem.
            Theorem: Physical charge lattice and Gram descent for K3 × E
            Let X = S × E , with S a K3 surface. The additive physical D-brane charge lattice in
            the even RR sector is
                                                             phys
                                                          ΓX        ​
                                                                        = H (S , Z) ⊕ H (S , Z).

                                                                              Γind ≅ Z3​




            is the image of the quadratic Gram map
                                                                                       Q2         P2

                                                                                       2          2

            For a rank-one D6–D2–D0 ideal-sheaf charge with curve class (β, d), Euler
            characteristic k, and β 2 = 2h − 2,
                                      vX (IY ) = (1, 0, 1 − d) ⊗ 1E + (0, −β, −k) ⊗ ωE .
                                           ​     ​                                                   ​                       ​




            Choosing the Igusa electric-magnetic pair

                                            QY = (0, −β, −k),
                                                  ​                                                 PY = (1, 0, 1 − d),
                                                                                                          ​




            one obtains
                                                      Π(QY , PY ) = (h − 1, k, d − 1).
                                                                   ​            ​




            Thus the OP/Borcherds monomial is exactly
                                             q h−1 rk sd−1 = exp(2πi tr(T (QY , PY )Z)).                                  ​               ​




            The map Π is not additive. Its failure is the explicit bilinear cocycle
                           B((Q1 , P1 ), (Q2 , P2 )) = (Q1 ⋅ Q2 , Q1 ⋅ P2 + Q2 ⋅ P1 , P1 ⋅ P2 ).

            10. The physics interpretation
            The two Künneth Mukai components
                                               Q = (0, −β, −k),                          P = (1, 0, 1 − d)

            are the two physical charge vectors whose Gram invariants are counted by the Igusa
            variables.
            The K3 curve norm gives
                                                                       Q2 /2 = h − 1.

            The D0/Fourier charge gives
                                                                             Q ⋅ P = k.

            The elliptic degree, with the Todd/vacuum shift, gives
                                                                       P 2 /2 = d − 1.

            So the Igusa triple is physically
                                                   Q2         P2
                                      (n, l, m) = ( , Q ⋅ P ,    ) = (h − 1, k, d − 1).
                                                   2          2
                                                                   ​                 ​                        ​




            This is exactly the same triple that appears in the Borcherds product
                                                               ∏ (1 − q n rl sm )f (nm,l) .
                                                                         ​




                                                             (n,l,m)>0

            The product does not live directly on the raw physical lattice. It lives on the primitive
            Gram quotient of the physical lattice.
            That is the construction.

                                                         I want it to be a dirac-inspired paper (as if it was written
                                                         by dirac) on the physics here, that makes natural the
                                                         factorisation algebra from physical first-principles etc.

            I’ll recast it in a Dirac style: begin with observables, charges, locality, and the protected
            supercharge, then derive factorization, Hall structure, and the Igusa square root.
            The crucial shift is that charged BPS states are not local fields; locality governs
            protected observables and collision laws before charge completion and trace reveal the
            automorphic product.
            Thought for 6m 51s
            Yes. The paper should be Dirac-inspired in exactly this sense:
               Do not begin with a scalar partition function and ask for a square root.
               Begin with a first-order physical object whose scalar shadow is a square.
            That is the Dirac move. Dirac did not take an analytic square root of the Klein–Gordon
            operator. He introduced new algebraic degrees of freedom — spinors and Clifford
            multiplication — such that the square became scalar. Your paper should do the same.
            The Igusa square root should not be presented as a branch of Δ25. It should be
            presented as the first-order protected chiral/Pfaffian object whose scalar reduced
                                                                                                       ​




            DT/OP theory is the square. Your current manuscript already gestures at this through
            protected locality, the E3-source, the orientation character, and the statement that the
            scalar square forgets the automorphic reflection sign. The rewrite should make that the
                                                    ​




            central physics rather than a caveat. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            1. States are not first. Observables are first.
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                    Attack Whitepaper Analysis




            A charged BPS state is not a local field. It is a sector. Its charge is measured at infinity.
            Therefore the microscopic Hilbert space is not the natural first object.
            The first object is the algebra of protected operations.
            A state is a representation of that algebra. A partition function is a trace of that
            representation. A trace cannot reconstruct the algebra. So the paper should not begin
            with the scalar trace and then apologize that it cannot reconstruct locality. It should
            begin with locality and then show what trace it produces.
            The physical hierarchy should be:
             local protected operations ⟶ factorization/Hall algebra ⟶ primitive Lie algebra

            This is already latent in your manuscript, but it should become the philosophical and
            technical backbone.

            2. The physical charge lattice
            Let
                                                              X = S × E,

            where S is a K3 surface.
            The even Mukai lattice of S is
                                    ΛS = H (S, Z) = H 0 (S, Z) ⊕ H 2 (S, Z) ⊕ H 4 (S, Z),

            with pairing
                                             (r, D, s) ⋅ (r′ , D′ , s′ ) = D ⋅ D′ − rs′ − r′ s.

            Since
                                               H even (X, Z) ≅ H (S, Z) ⊗ H even (E, Z),

            every even charge decomposes as
                                                  vX (F ) = P (F ) ⊗ 1E + Q(F ) ⊗ ωE .
                                                       ​                  ​                       ​

30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                       Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            Thus the additive physical charge lattice is
                                                                   Γphys
                                                                    X    = ΛS ⊕ ΛS ,
                                                                            ​                 ​                   ​   ​




            with charge
                                                                c(F ) = (Q(F ), P (F )).

            This is the lattice on which Hall multiplication is genuinely additive.
            The Igusa variables are not this lattice. They are the Gram shadow:
                                                           Q2         P2
                                               Π(Q, P ) = ( , Q ⋅ P ,    ) = (n, l, m).
                                                           2          2
                                                                        ​                                     ​               ​




            So the Fourier monomial is

            where
                                                                             Q2 /2   Q ⋅ P /2
                                                          T (Q, P ) = (                       ).
                                                                            Q ⋅ P /2  P 2 /2
                                                                                                      ​                   ​




            Thus the genus-two variable Z is a chemical potential for the rank-two charge plane
            spanned by Q and P .
            For the D6–D2–D0 ideal-sheaf sector,
                                      vX (IY ) = (1, 0, 1 − d) ⊗ 1E + (0, −β, −k) ⊗ ωE .

                                               PY = (1, 0, 1 − d),

            3. The local object on X
            The local object is not the scalar DT series. The local object is the protected open-string
            observable algebra on the Calabi–Yau threefold X .
            In the B-model language, the local field theory on a Calabi–Yau threefold is holomorphic
            Chern–Simons/BV theory. Locally, for a brane background F , the fields are controlled by
            the dg Lie algebra
                                                             gF (U ) = RHomU (F , F )[1]
                                                               ​                                           ​




            or, perturbatively,
                                                                       Ω0,∗
                                                                        c (U , End F )[1].
                                                                               ​




            The BV pairing comes from Serre duality and the holomorphic volume form on X . The
            classical interaction is cubic:
                                                                1      1
                                              S(A) = ∫ ΩX ∧ Tr ( A∂ˉA + A3 ) .
                                                                2      3
                                                                       ​           ​                           ​            ​




                                                      X

            The compact nonperturbative version replaces one fixed brane by the derived stack of
            compactly supported branes.
            For an open U ⊂ X , let
                                                                                       Mc ( U ) ​




            be the derived stack of compactly supported coherent B-branes in U with physical
            charge

            The Calabi–Yau threefold structure gives this stack a (−1)-shifted symplectic structure.

            the local critical model, and the completion is the physical charge/HN completion.
                          ​




            This is the object the paper should call the compact protected observable algebra.
            It is not yet a Hilbert space. It is a cohomological algebra of protected operations.

            4. Why this is naturally an E3 -factorization algebra                                                     ​




            The reason is purely local.
            Let U1, … , Uk ⊂ V ⊂ X be pairwise disjoint holomorphic opens. If
                      ​             ​




                                                                                  F i ∈ Mc i ( U i ) ,
                                                                                          ​

                                                                                                                                    ​
                                                                                                                                        ​               ​




            then
                                                        F1 ⊕ ⋯ ⊕ Fk ∈ Mc1 +⋯+ck (V ).
                                                             ​                                                    ​


                                                                                                                                                    ​                                ​
                                                                                                                                                                                         ​




            Because the supports are disjoint,
                                                        Ext∙ (Fi , Fj ) = 0       ​                   ​                                                                         (i =
                                                                                                                                                                                    j).

            Therefore the deformation-obstruction theory splits:
                                        RHom (⨁ Fi , ⨁ Fi ) ≃ ⨁ RHom(Fi , Fi ).
                                                                     ​    ​                           ​                   ​                                                      ​                   ​       ​




                                                                 i                    i                                                                         i

            The critical functions add, the vanishing cycles factor by Thom–Sebastiani, and the
            orientation lines multiply:
                                                                              Φ ⊕ i F i ≃ ⊠i Φ F i ,      ​
                                                                                                              ​                             ​

                                                                                                                                                                        ​
                                                                                                                                                                            ​




                                                                                  o ⊕ i F i ≃ ⊠i o F i .  ​
                                                                                                              ​                             ​

                                                                                                                                                                ​
                                                                                                                                                                    ​

            Hence one obtains factorization maps
                                                AX (U1 ) ⊗ ⋯ ⊗ AX (Uk ) ⟶ AX (V ).
                                                      ​                                   ​                           ​                   ​




            When the opens collide, the cross-Ext groups no longer vanish. Their nonzero
            extensions are exactly the Hall correspondences:
                                                   Mc 1 × Mc 2 ⟵ E c 1 , c 2 ⟶ Mc 1 + c 2 .
                                                             ​
                                                                 ​

                                                                            ​

                                                                                              ​       ​
                                                                                                          ​

                                                                                                                                      ​       ​
                                                                                                                                                  ​




            Thus disjointness gives ordinary factorization; collision gives the Hall product.
            This is the physical origin of the factorization algebra.
            The reason the structure is called holomorphic E3, rather than real E6, is that the
            protected supercharge has killed the anti-holomorphic translations. The remaining local
                                                                                                                              ​                           ​




            singularities depend on three complex holomorphic directions. A small holomorphic
            polydisc in X behaves like the local model for a holomorphic E3-algebra. With a choice
            of formality/framing, one obtains an ordinary E3-shadow.

            means:
               the protected local observables of the holomorphic Calabi–Yau threefold theory form
               a holomorphic factorization algebra whose pointwise local model is an E3-algebra
               after the chosen formality/framing.
                                                                                                                                                              ​




            This makes the E3 natural, not decorative.

            6. Locality fails exactly where it should fail
            Now the important point: positive elliptic degree is not local on E .
            Let C ⊂ S × E be a one-dimensional subscheme with positive elliptic degree:
                                                                (pE )∗ [C] = d[E],
                                                                      ​    ​                                           d > 0.

            Then pE (C) is a one-dimensional closed subset of the irreducible curve E . Hence
                         ​




                                                                                       pE (C) = E.
                                                                                           ​




            Therefore C cannot be assigned to finitely many points of E .
            This is not a mathematical inconvenience. It is the physics.
            A brane wrapping E is not a local insertion on E . It is a global sector, just as a Wilson
            loop is not a pointlike local observable in the direction it wraps. It can couple to local
            insertions, but it is not itself localized at their points.
            Therefore the correct object is not simply
                                                                               Fact(Ran(E)).

            It is a hybrid object:
                                                                  FXhyb = FXpf ↷ WX .

                                                                                          m>0

            is the wrapped elliptic-degree sector, with

            correspondences:

                                                ​    ​                                ​        ​                       ​               ​




            Wrapped-wrapped extensions give

            The interaction term is measured on the additive physical charge lattice before Gram
            descent.
            This is the physical replacement for the naive Ran(E) locality statement.
            The s-direction in the Igusa product is therefore not a local point-support direction on E
            . It is the wrapped/global direction. The factorization algebra survives, but it becomes
            stratified:
                      local vertical sector + global wrapped sector + mixed Hall action.                                                   ​




            That is the correct locality physics.

            9. Unitarity is used before the index, not after it
            The paper should be very explicit here.
            The untwisted theory is unitary. This matters because it gives an adjoint supercharge:
                                                                    Q† ,

            and an identity of the form
                                                             {Q, Q† } = Hright .      ​




            Then all states with positive right-moving energy pair and cancel in the protected
            supertrace. This is why the K3 elliptic genus localizes.
            So unitarity supplies the cancellation theorem.
            But after taking Q-cohomology, one no longer has a positive Hilbert space. One has a
            cohomological, charge-graded, virtual object.
            Thus
                                                                  f (n, l)

            is a superdimension, not a dimension.
            A negative coefficient means an odd excess:
                                           f (n, l) < 0       ⟺       dim H1ˉ − dim H0ˉ > 0
                                                                                  ​                    ​




            in whatever protected representative realizes it.
            The Borcherds denominator algebra is also not a unitary Hilbert-space representation.
            Its Cartan lattice is Lorentzian. Its multiplicities are signed supermultiplicities:
                                                     smult(α) = dim gα,0ˉ − dim gα,1ˉ .
                                                                             ​                     ​




            This is exactly what one expects from a protected cohomological theory.
            So the correct statement is:
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                 39/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                               Attack Whitepaper Analysis

             unitarity is an input to localization;                             the square root is a cohomological protected

            That is not a weakness. It is the nature of an index.

            11. The denominator algebra as the first-order equation
            In the Dirac equation, the first-order operator is not arbitrary. Its square gives the scalar
            operator because the gamma matrices obey a Clifford relation.
            Here the analogue is the Borcherds–Kac–Moody denominator algebra.

            14. Locality summary in one sentence

            3. The cocycle theorem
            For
                                                       c = (Q, P ),            c′ = (Q′ , P ′ ),

            define
                                        B(c, c′ ) = (Q ⋅ Q′ , Q ⋅ P ′ + Q′ ⋅ P , P ⋅ P ′ ) ∈ Γind .          ​   ​




            This is the polarization of the quadratic Gram map.
            Theorem 3.1 — B is a symmetric bilinear normalized 2-cocycle
            For all c, c′ , c′′ ∈ Γphys
                                   X ,          ​




                                                              B(c, c′ ) = B(c′ , c),

                                                    B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c, c′′ ) + B(c′ , c′′ ),

                                                    B(c, c′ + c′′ ) = B(c, c′ ) + B(c, c′′ ),

            and
                                                             B(c, 0) = B(0, c) = 0.

            Moreover,
                                                    Π(c + c′ ) = Π(c) + Π(c′ ) + B(c, c′ ).              ​




            Consequently B satisfies the additive 2-cocycle equation
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                           47/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                      Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                     B(c, c′ ) + B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c′ , c′′ ) + B(c, c′ + c′′ ).   ​




            Proof
            Write c = (Q, P ), c′ = (Q′ , P ′ ), c′′ = (Q′′, P ′′). Symmetry is immediate from
            symmetry of the lattice pairing.
            Bilinearity follows because each component of B is bilinear in (Q, P ) and (Q′ , P ′ ). For
            example,
                                                   (Q + Q′ ) ⋅ Q′′ = Q ⋅ Q′′ + Q′ ⋅ Q′′ ,

            and similarly for the other components.
            The Gram-defect identity follows from expanding:
                                                    (Q + Q′ )2   Q2   Q′2
                                                               =    +     + Q ⋅ Q′ ,
                                                        2        2     2
                                                                   ​   ​             ​




                               (Q + Q′ ) ⋅ (P + P ′ ) = Q ⋅ P + Q′ ⋅ P ′ + Q ⋅ P ′ + Q′ ⋅ P ,

                                                    (P + P ′ )2   P2   P ′2
                                                                =    +      + P ⋅ P ′.
                                                        2         2     2
                                                                   ​   ​             ​




            Thus
                                                 Π(c + c′ ) = Π(c) + Π(c′ ) + B(c, c′ ).

            Finally,
                               B(c, c′ ) + B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c, c′ ) + B(c, c′′ ) + B(c′ , c′′ ),

            while
                              B(c′ , c′′ ) + B(c, c′ + c′′ ) = B(c′ , c′′ ) + B(c, c′ ) + B(c, c′′ ).

            The two sides agree. ∎

            5. The key construction: the normal-ordered Gram map
            The raw Gram map
                                                                  Π : Γphys
                                                                       X    → Γind
                                                                               ​                ​




            is not additive. But the B -twisted extension carries a canonical additive replacement.
            Define
                                                                  Π : ΓX ⟶ Γind
                                                                          ​                 ​       ​




            by
                                                                Π(c, κ) = Π(c) − κ.                     ​




            Theorem 5.1 — Π is a group homomorphism
            For all (c, κ), (c′ , κ′ ) ∈ ΓX ,           ​




                                              Π((c, κ) ⋆ (c′ , κ′ )) = Π(c, κ) + Π(c′ , κ′ ).               ​




            Proof
            Using the definition of ⋆,
                                           (c, κ) ⋆ (c′ , κ′ ) = (c + c′ , κ + κ′ + B(c, c′ )).

            Therefore
                         Π((c, κ) ⋆ (c′ , κ′ )) = Π(c + c′ ) − κ − κ′ − B(c, c′ )
                                                = Π(c) + Π(c′ ) + B(c, c′ ) − κ − κ′ − B(c, c′ )
                                                = (Π(c) − κ) + (Π(c′ ) − κ′ )
                                                                                                                ​




                                                             = Π(c, κ) + Π(c′ , κ′ ).

            ∎

            This is the main repair.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                          50/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                  Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            The raw physical Gram is
                                                                             Π(c).

            The additive Borcherds/Iausa degree is
                                                                  Π(c, κ) = Π(c) − κ.

            The defect is
                                                                  κ = Π(c) − Π(c, κ).

            So a bound state can have physical charge c, actual Gram Π(c), and normal-ordered
            Borcherds degree Π(c, κ). The difference is precisely the accumulated pairwise
            interaction term.

            10. Corrected descent theorem
            The right descent statement is this.
            Definition 10.1 — B -normal-ordered primitive Hall algebra
            A B -normal-ordered compact primitive Hall algebra is a Lie superalgebra
                                                                                  PX = ⨁ (PX )c
                                                                                              ​                                        ​               ​       ​




                                                                                                              c∈ΓX                 ​




            such that
                                                                 [(PX )c , (PX )c′ ] ⊆ (PX )c⋆c′ .
                                                                              ​       ​                       ​        ​                                           ​                   ​




            The Borcherds/Iausa degree of a homogeneous element in degree c is
                                                                                          Π(c) ∈ Γind .                                    ​




            Define the normal-ordered pushforward
                                                                  (Π∗ PX )γ = ⨁ (PX )c .
                                                                              ​           ​               ​                                    ​                       ​           ​       ​




                                                                                                                      Π(c)=γ


            Theorem 10.2 — Normal-ordered pushforward is automatically a
            Γind -graded Lie superalgebra

            If PX is ΓX -graded, then
                    ​         ​




                                                       [Π∗ PX γ , Π∗ PX γ ′ ] ⊆ Π∗ PX γ+γ ′ .
                                                                  ​       ​       ​               ​               ​        ​                               ​                   ​               ​       ​




            Proof
            Let

            13. Pfaffian/orientation theorem
            Now make the Dirac/Pfaffian part precise.
            For a compact Calabi–Yau threefold X , Joyce–Upmeier prove natural orientation data
            for moduli stacks of coherent sheaves and perfect complexes; in their formulation,
            orientation data is a square root of the natural canonical-type line bundle compatible
            with short exact sequences. Since K3 × E is a compact Calabi–Yau threefold, this
            gives the correct general existence input for an unreduced CY3 sheaf/perfect-complex
            orientation datum. arXiv
            The Borisov–Joyce and Oh–Thomas theories are closely related Pfaffian/orientation
            technologies in the CY4 setting: Borisov–Joyce construct virtual classes for proper
            oriented (−2)-shifted symplectic derived schemes, and Oh–Thomas construct
            algebraic CY4 virtual cycles using localized square-root Euler classes. These are
            important analogues of the square-root/Pfaffian mechanism, but they do not by
            themselves prove the reduced K3 × E Hall orientation monodromy. arXiv +1
            Theorem 13.1 — Orientation monodromy is a character
            Assume a compact Hall/factorization realization supplies an orientation line
                                                                     o

            with
                                                             o⊗2 ≃ D,

            where D is the determinant/virtual canonical line of the protected critical theory.
            Assume a lifted action of the type-II Weyl group
                                                             W (2) (Λ2,1
                                                                     II )

            on the oriented completed Hall object. For each w ∈ W (2) (Λ2,1
                                                                        II ), suppose the lift acts
            on o by a sign
                                                                                                  ​

30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                                         Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                                   w o ≃ ϵo (w)o,                    ​                                                         ϵo (w) ∈ {±1}.

                                                                         ​




                                                                              II ) → {±1}                                                  ​                                            ​




            is a group character.
            Proof

            If
                                                                                             w1∗ o ≃ ϵo (w1 )o
                                                                                                             ​                         ​                        ​




            and
                                                                                         w2∗ o ≃ ϵo (w2 )o,
                                                                                                     ​                             ​                    ​




            then
                                                          (w1 w2 )∗ o ≃ ϵo (w1 )ϵo (w2 )o.

            Thus

            ∎

            3. The E -equivariant descent lemma is currently wrong
            Lemma 5.8 is the most dangerous mathematical section in the draft. It says the
            equivariant Serre spectral sequence has
                                                                 H 1 (BE; Z/2) ≅ (Z/2)2

            and then writes
                                      H 2 (BE; Z/2) = Z/2⟨αβ⟩ ⊕ Z/2⟨α2 ⟩ ⊕ Z/2⟨β 2 ⟩.

            That is the cohomology pattern of B(Z/2)2, not of BE for E top ≅ T 2. For a
            connected torus,
                                                                 BE ≃ BT 2 ≃ K(Z2 , 2),

            so
                                               H ∗ (BE; F2 ) = F2 [u1 , u2 ],
                                                                        ​                           ​       ​           ​   ∣ui ∣ = 2.
                                                                                                                                ​




            Hence
                                                                       H 1 (BE; F2 ) = 0,                           ​




            and
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                               70/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                         Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                                                       H (BE; F2 ) ≅ (F2 )2 ,
                                                                         2
                                                                                           ​




            not (F2)3. The (F2)3 degree-two group belongs to
                        ​                 ​




                                                  H 2 (BE[2]; F2 ),               ​                        E[2] ≅ (Z/2)2 ,

            which is a different object. The draft later correctly treats E[2] as Klein-four, but the
            free connected E -quotient and the finite stabilizer E[2]-quotient are being mixed.
                    raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…                    raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            Fix
            Split Lemma 5.8 into two independent results.
            Lemma 5.8A: Free connected E -descent
            For the free E -locus,
                    E2p,q = H p (BE; H q (MC ; F2 )),
                            ​                                 ​          ​                H ∗ (BE; F2 ) = F2 [u1 , u2 ],
                                                                                                                       ​               ​   ​   ​   ∣ui ∣ = 2.
                                                                                                                                                      ​




            There is no E21,1 obstruction from H 1(BE), because H 1(BE) = 0. The first
            connected equivariant obstruction lives in total degree two through
                                     ​




                                                  E22,0 = H 2 (BE; F2 ) ⊗ H 0 (MC ; F2 ),
                                                         ​                                     ​                               ​   ​




            and in
                                                             0,2
                                                       E2 = H 0 (BE; H 2 (MC ; F2 )).
                                                                   ​                                               ​       ​




            So the descent obstruction must be rewritten as a class in
                                                                             HE2 (MC ; F2 ),
                                                                                      ​                ​       ​




            with connected-torus part lying in F2u1 ⊕ F2u2, not in α2, αβ, β 2.
                                                                              ​   ​                ​       ​




            Lemma 5.8B: Finite stabilizer E[N ]-descent
            On small-orbit strata, the stabilizer is finite:
                                                                         E[N ] ≅ (Z/N )2 .

            For N = 2,

            4. The small-orbit gerbe vanishing is not proved

            automatically trivialize the orientation gerbe on the moduli stack. You must compute the
                                                                                                                                ​




            equivariant determinant line, not only the ordinary determinant line.
            The claim
                                                                              E,E[2]
                                                                             βC        ​   =0

            may be true on a rank-one stratum, but the present proof does not establish it.

            6. The B -cocycle material is strong, but the descent
            should be normal-ordered rather than “shifted away”
            The charge-lattice upgrade is the best new mathematics in the draft. You now define
                                                                       phys
                                                                  ΓX          ​
                                                                                      = ΛS ⊕ ΛS ,
                                                                                              ​           ​




                                                   ΠX (Q, P ) = (Q2 /2, Q ⋅ P , P 2 /2) ,
                                                            ​




            and
                                           B(c, c′ ) = (Q ⋅ Q′ , Q ⋅ P ′ + Q′ ⋅ P , P ⋅ P ′ ).

            The draft proves
                                             ΠX (c + c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ) + B(c, c′ ),
                                                   ​                              ​                   ​




            and the central product
                                          (c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ ) = (c + c′ , T + T ′ + B(c, c′ )).

            This is correct and important. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…
            But the language “subtracting B(c, c′ )” by a cochain Θ can sound like you are forcing
            non-additive physics into additive Gram degrees by fiat. The safer and stronger
            construction is normal-ordering.
            Define
                                                                ΓX = Γphys
                                                                   ​




                                                                      X    ⊕B Γgram .    ​        ​           ​




            Then define the additive normal-ordered Gram map
                                            Π : ΓX → Γgram ,
                                                        ​                ​                   Π(c, T ) = Π(c) − T .

            Because
                                                 Π(c + c′ ) = Π(c) + Π(c′ ) + B(c, c′ ),
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                           74/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                  Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            one has
                                             Π((c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ )) = Π(c, T ) + Π(c′ , T ′ ).

            So the correct theorem is:
                If the protected primitive Hall algebra is ΓX -graded and its bracket respects ⋆, then
                its normal-ordered pushforward by Π is a Γgram -graded Lie superalgebra.
                                                                                       ​




                                                                                               ​




            This avoids having to assert that a mysterious degree-shift isomorphism exists. The Θ-
            formalism can remain, but it should be presented as one possible categorified
            implementation of normal-ordering, not as a proven operation.
            The draft already has a conditional Θ-descent theorem and says it does not erase B ; it
            transports the raw target to the additive Gram target. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc… That
            is conceptually right. Make it mathematically cleaner by putting ΓX and Π first, then Θ
            as a categorification.
                                                                                                                       ​




            7. Add a no-go theorem for raw Π-descent
            This would be a major level-up.
            The paper should prove that raw Π-descent cannot realize the full BKM bracket. That
            makes your cocycle-corrected descent not optional but forced.
            Let P = ⨁c Pc be physically graded:
                                 ​    ​




                                                                       [Pc , Pc′ ] ⊂ Pc+c′ .
                                                                          ​    ​                       ​




            Raw Π-descent would require
                                                                 Π(c + c′ ) = Π(c) + Π(c′ )

            for every nonzero bracket. Therefore it requires
                                                                         B(c, c′ ) = 0

            for every nonzero bracket.
            But in the BKM algebra, for distinct real simple roots ei , ej , one has                       ​       ​




                                                     [ei , ej ] =
                                                             ​   0,
                                                                   ​               [ei , [ei , ej ]] =
                                                                                           ​       ​  0,      ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                 75/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                            Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            while
                                                                             (ad ei )3 ej = 0.       ​           ​




            If ci is a physical lift of ei , then the second bracket forces
                    ​                                  ​




                                                                     B(ci , ci + cj ) = 0.
                                                                                     ​           ​                   ​




            If also the first bracket forced B(ci , cj ) = 0, then       ​       ​




                                         B(ci , ci + cj ) = B(ci , ci ) + B(ci , cj ) = B(ci , ci ).
                                                   ​       ​   ​                             ​           ​                   ​   ​        ​   ​




            But
                                                                   B(ci , ci ) = 2Π(ci ) =
                                                                             ​            0.
                                                                                         ​                               ​




            Contradiction.
            Therefore:
                               raw Π-descent cannot realize the full real-root BKM strings.                                                       ​




            This theorem would be genuinely valuable. It proves that the Dirac/normal-ordering data
            is structurally necessary.

            elementary identity, and worse, that parity choices could change the Gram polarization.

            Current abstract is strong but still too long and theorem-heavy. Suggested replacement:
               For X = K3 × E , the protected K3 index satisfies ZK3 = 2ϕ0,1. Replacing it by
               the Borcherds half ϕ0,1 gives a K0-level virtual determinant, not a microscopic
                                                                                                             ​          ​




               Hilbert space. We prove that the theta-normalized determinant is
                                                         ​                 ​




                                         DX (Z) = 64q 1/2 r1/2 s1/2 ∏(1 − q n rl sm )f (nm,l) = Δ5 (Z).
                                                     ​                                    ​                                 ​




                                                                               Γeff   ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                          102/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                              Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                    The same coefficients are the signed root supermultiplicities of the Gritsenko–Nikulin
                    denominator algebra gΔ , with      5   ​

                                                                                   ​

                    The reduced OP/DT scalar branch gives
                                                                       X
