                                                                                     5 ,   ​                        ​




                    but this scalar square forgets the Maass reflection character.
                    The central structural problem is physical: a compact Hall/factorization realization is
                    not graded by Z3 directly. The additive physical lattice is
                                                                   phys
                                                        ΓX                ​
                                                                              = H (K3, Z) ⊕ H (K3, Z),

                    and the Igusa variables are the quadratic Gram shadow
                                                       Π(Q, P ) = (Q2 /2, Q ⋅ P , P 2 /2) .

                    We isolate the exact additivity defect
                                               B(c, c′ ) = (Q ⋅ Q′ , Q ⋅ P ′ + Q′ ⋅ P , P ⋅ P ′ )

                    and construct the normal-ordered extension ΓX = Γphys      ⊕B Γgram on which the
                    corrected Gram map is additive. Raw Π-descent cannot realize the full BKM real-root
                                                                            X                             ​                 ​   ​   ​




                    strings; cocycle-corrected descent is forced.
                    The square root is therefore meant in Dirac’s sense: the open problem is to construct
                    a first-order, orientation-sensitive protected algebra whose Pfaffian is Δ5, and
                    whose orientation-forgetting scalar square is the OP/DT branch.

            29. Proof-hygiene fixes
            29.1 Avoid proof sketches where theorem is strong
            If a result says “vanishes,” give a proof. If proof is incomplete, call it a criterion or
            conjecture.
            29.2 Separate algebraic proof from geometric assumption
            For example:
                algebraic: B is a cocycle.
                geometric: Hall bracket realizes B -shift by Θ.
            Do not put both under one theorem unless assumptions are explicit.
            29.3 Do not use “standard” for nonstandard composites
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                              116/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                            Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            “Standard equivariant Serre spectral sequence” is fine.
            “Standard equivariant derived setting of reduced K3 × E Hall stack with cosection and
            orientation descent” is not standard.
            29.4 Reduce citation stacking
            Several paragraphs list many orientation references. Instead:
                one paragraph says what exists in known CY3/CY4 literature,
                next paragraph says what is not covered here.
            29.5 Check dimensional language
            Replace every “fourfold” reference to K3 × E with “threefold.”

            30. Physics-strength upgrades
            30.1 Add a “Dirac principle” section
            Suggested statement:
               A scalar square is not square-rooted by choosing a branch. It is square-rooted by
               finding a first-order object with extra algebraic degrees of freedom. In this paper
               those degrees of freedom are orientation, primitive bracket, and normal-ordered
               charge descent.
            30.2 Add “unitarity before index, not after”
            The draft already says this. Make it a boxed principle:
             unitarity proves localization; the localized object is cohomological and virtual.                ​




            30.3 Add “states vs observables”
                    BPS states are superselection sectors. The local/factorization object is the algebra of
                    protected operations on sectors.
            30.4 Add “wrapped sectors are line defects over E ”
            A positive elliptic-degree brane should be physically compared to a wrapped/global
            operator, not a point insertion on E .
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                        117/496
            30.5 Add “wall crossing”
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                      Attack Whitepaper Analysis




            State that the compact Hall category must be stability-chamber dependent, while Δ5 is
            an automorphic object whose chamber expansion is selected by the cusp.
                                                                                                                                ​




            31. Mathematics-strength upgrades
            31.1 Prove the raw descent no-go theorem
            This is probably the most publishable new lemma beyond the cocycle itself.
            31.2 Prove primitive lift/surjectivity of Π
            Easy, useful, clarifies physical geometry.
            31.3 Formalize ΓX -graded Lie algebras
                                                 ​




            Define the category:
                                                                         LieSAlgΓX              ​
                                                                                                    ​




            and the pushforward functor:
                                                         Π∗ : LieSAlgΓX → LieSAlgΓgram .
                                                             ​




                                                                               ​
                                                                                   ​




                                                                                                                    ​
                                                                                                                        ​




            31.4 Turn Θ-descent into a theorem about functors
            At chain level:
                                          Θc,c′ : Pc+c′ [Π(c + c′ )] → Pc+c′ [Π(c) + Π(c′ )]
                                                     ​           ​                                          ​




            with coherence:

            Construction 3: Primitive lift of every Gram triple

            Construction 4: Gram defect cocycle
            Define
                                            B(c, c′ ) = (Q ⋅ Q′ , Q ⋅ P ′ + Q′ ⋅ P , P ⋅ P ′ )                     ​




            for
                                                        c = (Q, P ),               c′ = (Q′ , P ′ ).

            Then
                                             ΠX (c + c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ) + B(c, c′ ).
                                                   ​                    ​                      ​               ​




            The proof is the polarization identity:
                                                       (Q + Q′ )2   Q2   Q′2
                                                                  =    +     + Q ⋅ Q′ ,
                                                           2        2     2
                                                                    ​          ​                   ​




                               (Q + Q′ ) ⋅ (P + P ′ ) = Q ⋅ P + Q′ ⋅ P ′ + Q ⋅ P ′ + Q′ ⋅ P ,

                                                       (P + P ′ )2   P2   P ′2
                                                                   =    +      + P ⋅ P ′.
                                                           2         2     2
                                                                    ​          ​                   ​




            Also
                                                              B(c, c) = 2ΠX (c).           ​




            Since B is symmetric bilinear, it is a normalized additive 2-cocycle:
                                     B(c, c′ ) + B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c′ , c′′ ) + B(c, c′ + c′′ ).

            What follows is the cleanest way to make sense of your two candidate-new theorems,

            1. The core correction to your anatomy
            Your anatomy is almost right, but it needs one surgical correction.
            You write:
                 The single block is hypothesis (P) — the compact PTVV host. Everything else either
                 reduces to (P) once supplied, or is finite-stage testable.
            That is true for the normal-ordered Π-descent theorem if (P ) is understood broadly
            enough to include the Hall/factorization host, quotient compatibility, finite HN topology,
            chain-level extension stacks, cyclic model, and orientation transport.
            It is not true for the Dirac–Pfaffian recognition theorem unless (P ) is enlarged so
            much that it includes (O1), (O1)+, and (O2). The Pfaffian theorem has independent
            geometric kernels:
                                          (O1) strong reduced quotient orientation,           ​




                                (O1)+           Weyl-equivariant determinant-line transport,          ​




                           (O2) local Pfaffian normal form on the three type-II walls.                    ​




            The latest draft now recognizes this separation: the D0-certificate supplies the first-
            order/Pfaffian finite-stage package only if its finite stacks, transition maps, quotient
            orientations, normal-ordering shifts, first-order complex, and Pfaffian windows are
            supplied; it explicitly states that these are not constructed by the lattice calculation,
            denominator algebra, or OP scalar square. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…
            So the precise dependency graph is:
                          Π-descent = (P ) + (E) + (O) + (Θ) + (F ) + (Cyc) + (Rad).                          ​




                                      Dirac–Pfaffian = (D0) + (O1) + (O1)+ + (O2).                ​

            4.3 Gram defect cocycle

            for

                                                   ​                                 ​                ​                   ​

                                                                  =    +     + Q ⋅ Q′ ,
                                                           2        2     2
                                                                         ​               ​                ​




                               (Q + Q′ ) ⋅ (P + P ′ ) = Q ⋅ P + Q′ ⋅ P ′ + Q ⋅ P ′ + Q′ ⋅ P ,

                                                       (P + P ′ )2   P2   P ′2
                                                                   =    +      + P ⋅ P ′.
                                                           2         2     2
                                                                         ​               ​                ​




            Thus B is the polarization of ΠX . It is symmetric bilinear and satisfies
                                                                 ​




                                     B(c, c′ ) + B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c′ , c′′ ) + B(c, c′ + c′′ ).

            4.4 Normal-ordered charge extension
            Define
                                                             ΓX = Γphys
                                                                   X ​  ⊕B Γgram                ​       ​   ​




            with product

            Then ΓX is an abelian group. The identity is
                             ​




                                                                             (0, 0),

            and the inverse is
                                                       (c, T )−1 = (−c, −T + B(c, c)).

            Define the normal-ordered Gram map
                                           ΠX : ΓX → Γgram ,
                                              ​        ​                 ​         ΠX (c, T ) = ΠX (c) − T .
                                                                                        ​                       ​               ​




            Then
                                           ΠX ((c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ )) = ΠX (c, T ) + ΠX (c′ , T ′ ).
                                                                                            ​                       ​       ​




            Proof:
                                 ΠX ((c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ )) = ΠX (c + c′ ) − T − T ′ − B(c, c′ )
                                       ​                                       ​




                                      = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ) + B(c, c′ ) − T − T ′ − B(c, c′ )
                                                  ​              ​




                                                      = (ΠX (c) − T ) + (ΠX (c′ ) − T ′ ).
                                                             ​                                      ​




            This is the clean mathematical heart of the paper. The latest manuscript has this
            extension  and explicitly says the compact Hall object, if constructed, is graded upstairs

                 phys

            This gives the physical meaning of the correction: the raw physical bound-state charge
            is ∑i ci , whose Gram matrix contains all pairwise cross-terms; the Borcherds/Fock
            degree is the normal-ordered sum of one-particle Gram degrees.

            4.6 Raw-descent no-go theorem
            Let
                                                                                     P = ⨁ Pc                               ​           ​




                                                                                                  phys
                                                                                               c∈ΓX                     ​




            be a physical-charge-graded Lie superalgebra with
                                                                             [Pc , Pc′ ] ⊆ Pc+c′ .
                                                                                      ​            ​                                            ​




            Raw ΠX -pushforward is Γgram -graded if and only if every nonzero bracket satisfies
                                 ​                                       ​




                                                                                     B(c, c′ ) = 0.

            But in gΔ , the real simple roots have Cartan matrix
                                     5   ​
                                             ​




                                                                              2 −2 −2
                                                                             −2 2 −2
                                                                             ​            ​                 ​                       ​               ​
                                                                                                                                                        .
                                                                             −2 −2 2

            For distinct real simple roots, the real-root string has nonzero brackets in degrees
                                                                                              γi + γj
                                                                                               ​                    ​




            and
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                                                                      162/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                              Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                                                           2γi + γj .      ​                    ​




            If ci , cj lift γi , γj , the first bracket forces
                    ​   ​       ​           ​




                                                                  B(ci , cj ) = 0. ​            ​




            The second bracket forces
                                                             B(ci , ci + cj ) = 0.
                                                                       ​               ​                    ​




            But
                               B(ci , ci + cj ) = B(ci , ci ) + B(ci , cj ) = 2ΠX (ci ) = 2γi =
                                        ​       ​   ​        ​     ​                           0.      ​               ​       ​       ​               ​




            Contradiction.
            Therefore
                            raw Gram descent cannot realize the full BKM real-root strings.                                                                 ​




            The normal-ordered extension is not cosmetic; it is forced.

                                                                               ​                        ​                         ​




            This is the finite-stage Hall source. Its degree law is literally
                                                         c + c′                    replaced by                                c ⋆ c′ .

            Your anatomy is the right research program, but the wording must be disciplined:

            hybrid wrapped source, Koszul source coalgebra, and primitive recognition data are

            1. Physical charge lattice

            The additive physical charge lattice is not the Igusa degree lattice Z3. It is the algebraic
            even dyonic Mukai lattice.
            Let
                                    ΛS = H (S, Z) = H 0 (S, Z) ⊕ H 2 (S, Z) ⊕ H 4 (S, Z),

                                               (r, D, s) ⋅ (r′ , D′ , s′ ) = D ⋅ D′ − rs′ − r′ s.

            2. Normal-ordered charge extension
            Define the symmetric bilinear polarization defect
                                               B(c, c′ ) = (Q ⋅ Q′ , Q ⋅ P ′ + Q′ ⋅ P , P ⋅ P ′ )                                                 ​




            for
                                                        c = (Q, P ),                                       c′ = (Q′ , P ′ ).

                                               ΠX (c + c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ) + B(c, c′ ).
                                                   ​                                 ​                                ​                       ​




            Also
                                                                  B(c, c) = 2ΠX (c).                              ​           ​

                                                                 ΓX = Γphys
                                                                       X
                                                                       ​    ⊕B Γgram .             ​          ​                   ​   ​

            Elements are written

                                          (c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ ) = (c + c′ , T + T ′ + B(c, c′ )).                                                                             ​




            The identity is
                                                                                                    (0, 0),

            and the inverse is
                                                                   (c, T )−1 = (−c, −T + B(c, c)).

            Define

            Then
                                                                       ΠX (c ⋆ c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ).

            This is the core construction. It is not an auxiliary fix. It is the only way the physical Hall

            3. Why normal-ordering must be source-built
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                                        Attack Whitepaper Analysis




            Raw ΠX -descent cannot realize the BKM bracket.
                         ​




            Suppose
                                                                                 P = ⨁ Pc                                                    ​       ​




                                                                                                                             c

            is a physically graded primitive Hall Lie superalgebra with
                                                                              [Pc , Pc′ ] ⊆ Pc+c′ .
                                                                                         ​                       ​                                           ​




            Raw ΠX -pushforward would require every nonzero bracket to satisfy
                         ​




                                                      ΠX (c + c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ),
                                                                 ​                                                                       ​                                   ​




            hence
                                                                                B(c, c′ ) = 0.

            But the real-root strings of gΔ require, for distinct real simple generators ei , ej ,
                                                             5       ​
                                                                         ​                                                                                                               ​   ​




                                                                                 [ei , ej ] =
                                                                                             0, ​                       ​




                                                                              [ei , [ei , ej ]] =
                                                                                     ​
                                                                                                 0.         ​                       ​




            The first bracket would force
                                                                               B(ci , cj ) = 0.          ​                   ​




            The second would force
                                                                             B(ci , ci + cj ) = 0.
                                                                                             ​               ​                                   ​




            But
                                   B(ci , ci + cj ) = B(ci , ci ) + B(ci , cj ) = 2ΠX (ci ) =
                                           ​    ​        ​                       ​           0.     ​                                                   ​           ​           ​   ​




            Contradiction.
            So the corrected source is not a raw physical-charge Hall algebra followed by a later
            quotient. The corrected source must be a ΓX -graded Hall/factorization object from the
            beginning:
                                                                                                                                 ​




                                                                             [Hc , Hc′ ] ⊆ Hc⋆c′ .
                                                                                         ​                           ​                                           ​       ​

            11. Hall state spaces
            Define the finite state space
                                                 HR,c = H∗BM (MR,c /E, ΦR,c ⊗ oR,c ) .
                                                         ​                              ​                                                ​                                                                ​   ​




            The total finite state object is
                                                                  HR = ⨁ HR,c .     ​                                                            ​                       ​                ​




                                                                                                      c∈ΓX,R                                 ​




            For a correspondence
                                                                                                     p                                                           q
                                                       Mc × Mc ′
                                                             ​                              ​                            ​       Ec, c′                      ​               ​       Mc ⋆ c ′ ,       ​




            define the product
                                                                  μR = q! ∘ TS ∘p∗ .
                                                                            ​                                        ​                                                                ​




            Here TS is the Thom–Sebastiani map
                                                                  Φc ⊠ Φc′ → Φc⋆c′
                                                                                ​                                                ​                                               ​




            together with multiplicative orientation transport
                                                                   oc ⊠ oc ′ → oc ⋆ c ′ .
                                                                            ​                                                ​                                       ​




            The coproduct is
                                                                 ΔR = p! ∘ TS−1 ∘q ∗ .
                                                                        ​                                    ​                                                                                    ​

30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                                          Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            The primitive subspace is
                                                   PR = ker (ΔR − id ⊗ 1 − 1 ⊗ id) .
                                                        ​                                                     ​                                                                                     ​




            The bracket is the supercommutator
                                               [x, y]R = μR (x, y) − (−1)∣x∣∣y∣ μR (y, x).
                                                             ​                            ​                                                                                         ​                   ​




            Since
                                                        μR : HR,c ⊗ HR,c′ → HR,c⋆c′ ,
                                                                     ​                                                                  ​                                                   ​




            the primitive bracket is automatically ΓX -graded.                                            ​




            12. Normal-ordered pushforward
            Define
                                                                         (PRΠ )γ = ⨁ (PR )c .
                                                                                      ​           ​                                         ​           ​           ​       ​




                                                                                                                      ΠX (c)=γ  ​




            Because ΠX is a group homomorphism,
                                ​




                                                       ΠX (c ⋆ c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ),
                                                                 ​                                                                  ​                                   ​




            the bracket descends:
                                                            [(PRΠ )γ , (PRΠ )γ ′ ] ⊆ (PRΠ )γ+γ ′ .
                                                                              ​   ​                               ​         ​                       ​                                   ​       ​




            This makes ΘΠ concrete. ​




            At chain level, introduce central translation functors
                                                             Tη : HR,(c,T ) → HR,(c,T +η) .
                                                                         ​                                             ​                                                        ​




            Then
                                                                             Bch (c, c′ ) = TB(c,c′ ) .
                                                                                              ​                                                                 ​




            A normal-ordering cochain is
                                                                             ΘΠ,R (c) = T−ΠX (c) .    ​



                                                                                                                                                ​
                                                                                                                                                            ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                                                                                  204/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                       Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            Its Hochschild boundary is
                             dHoch ΘΠ,R (c, c′ ) = −ΠX (c) − ΠX (c′ ) + ΠX (c + c′ ) = B(c, c′ ).
                                    ​         ​                                                 ​                               ​




            Thus
                                                                 dHoch ΘΠ,R = Bch .
                                                                            ​               ​                   ​       ​




            The triple pentagon is exactly
                                            B(a, b) + B(a + b, c) = B(b, c) + B(a, b + c).

            Thus the normal-ordering obstruction classes are killed by construction at the degree
            level; the remaining checks are geometric compatibility with correspondences,
            orientations, cyclic structures, and radicals. The latest draft lists these seven classes:

            13. How NO1–NO7 vanish in the constructed source
            The source-built normal-ordering kills the obstruction package as follows.
            NO1: finite-fibre descent
            At finite R, ΓX,R is finite. Therefore every fibre
                                        ​




                                                                   Π−1
                                                                    X (γ) ∩ ΓX,R​                           ​




            is finite.
            So ΠΘR,∗ is finite.
                         ​




            NO2: Hochschild boundary
            By construction,
                                                                 dHoch ΘΠ,R = Bch,R .
                                                                        ​               ​                           ​




            NO3: triple pentagon
            Associativity of ⋆ is equivalent to
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                              205/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                         B(a, b) + B(a + b, c) = B(b, c) + B(a, b + c).

            Thus the degree pentagon is strict.
            The geometric pentagon is the equality of pull-push maps over the eight finite two-step
            flag stacks.
            NO4: Jacobi
            The Hall product is associative over finite two-step flags. Therefore its supercommutator
            satisfies Jacobi after passing to primitives.
            NO5: Frobenius
            The CY3 Serre pairing gives
                                                   ⟨[x, y], z⟩ + (−1)∣x∣∣y∣ ⟨y, [x, z]⟩ = 0.

            This is the Frobenius obstruction
                                                                         oF = 0.
                                                                             ​




            It follows once the protected category is cyclic Calabi–Yau of dimension 3, the primitive
            bracket is represented by the antisymmetrized Hall product, and the trace/pairing
            commute with finite R-transitions.
            NO6: negative cyclic lift
            The S 1-equivariant cyclic enhancement requires
                                                             (b + uBC )Θ−      −
                                                                        Π,R = Bch,R .
                                                                         ​                ​          ​   ​




            The Loday–Quillen–Tsygan comparison gives the Hochschild shadow after forgetting
            the S 1-structure.
            NO7: radical stability
            Let
                                                                         RadR         ​




            be the radical of the completed Hopf pairing. Require
                                                              [RadR , PR ] ⊆ RadR ,
                                                                     ​           ​               ​




            and
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                    206/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                         Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                              ΔR (RadR ) ⊆ RadR ⊗PR + PR ⊗ RadR .
                                                    ​            ​                                    ​                          ​               ​                               ​




            Then the radical quotient remains a Lie bialgebra/Hopf primitive object.

            16. Orientation-monodromy character
            The Weyl group of the type-II chamber is generated by the three real simple reflections
                                                                      sδ1 , sδ2 , sδ3 .
                                                                                  ​
                                                                                      ​


                                                                                               ​
                                                                                                   ​


                                                                                                                   ​
                                                                                                                       ​

                                                                δ1 = 2f2 − f3 ,
                                                                          ​                            ​                       ​




                                                                δ2 = 2f−2 − f3 ,
                                                                  ​                                            ​                                       ​

30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                                                     Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                                                                                          δ3 = f3 .
                                                                                                                  ​                                 ​




            The Pfaffian wall model requires, for each δi , a local wall coordinate ui such that                                  ​                                                     ​




                                                                                 sδi (ui ) = −ui .​
                                                                                                      ​                   ​                                     ​




            The reduced determinant complex splits as
                                                                       Kired ≃ Kitan ⊕ Kinor ,
                                                                                  ​                                                            ​                        ​




            with
                                                                       Kinor ≃ [R                                                                               R] .
                                                                                                                                                   ui   ​

                                                                                 Pf(Kinor ) = ui                                           ​                        ​




            up to invariant unit. Therefore
                                                                                 s∗δi Pf = − Pf .
                                                                                              ​
                                                                                                  ​




            Hence
                                                         ϵo (sδi ) = −1
                                                              ​


                                                                         ​
                                                                             ​
                                                                                                                                                        i = 1, 2, 3.                ​




            The type-II Weyl group has abelianization
                                                                      W (2) (Λ2,1           3
                                                                              II )ab ≃ (Z/2) .                        ​                ​




            Thus the signs on the three generators determine a character. Since the Maass
            character satisfies
                                                                                 νΔ5 (sδi ) = −1          ​
                                                                                                              ​


                                                                                                                              ​

                                                                                                                                                           II ).            ​   ​




            The latest draft correctly insists that this is geometric orientation monodromy, not scalar
            normalization: constructing the domain of the comparison and the character is part of
            the data, and the sign is not obtained by choosing the scalar normalization of Δ5.                                                                                              ​

            17. Primitive recognition
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                     Attack Whitepaper Analysis




            Define
                                                PXΠ = H 0 Primprot (ΠΘ
                                                     ​




                                                                     X,∗ FX ) / Rad .
                                                                          hyb
                                                                                                      ​           ​   ​




            A full recognition certificate is the data of:
             1. Cartan identification:
                                                             (PXΠ )0 ≅ Λ2,1
                                                                        II ⊗ C.
             2. Real simple representatives:
                                                                 ​        ​                               ​




                                                                         e i , f i , hi
                                                                                  ​                       ​




                    in degrees
                                                                     δ1 , δ2 , δ3 .
            3. Imaginary simple-root fibres with the Gritsenko–Nikulin multiplicity and parity data.
                                                                              ​           ​           ​




            4. Chevalley relations.
            5. Real Serre relations.
            6. Imaginary orthogonality relations.
            7. Generation by Cartan plus simple primitives.
            8. Completed PBW.
            9. Equality of the closed Hopf-pairing radical quotient.
           10. No-extra-relations.
           11. Full root-space parity dimensions, not merely signed dimensions.
           Then
                                                              PXΠ ≅ gΔ5 .
                                                                     ​

                                                                                              ​
                                                                                                  ​           ​




            The determinant alone cannot prove this. A graded super vector space with the same
            signed dimensions and zero bracket would have the same product determinant but the
            wrong algebra. The latest draft says exactly that the recognition certificate requires
            primitive representatives, relations, pairing, radical quotient, no-extra-relations,
            completed PBW, and parity data; it is not reconstructed from the determinant.
                    raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            20. New content to add to the manuscript
            The work we just did clarifies several new insertions.
            20.1 Source-built normal ordering
            Add a definition:
               The normal-ordered Hall source is not a raw Γphys X -graded Hall object plus a later
               correction. It is a ΓX -graded Hall object whose product lands in c ⋆ c′ . Thus the
                                                                                                                                           ​




               correction B(c, c′ ) is part of the target label of the extension stack.
                                                    ​




            This eliminates the impression that ΘΠ is an arbitrary shift.                 ​




            20.2 Anchor via determinant of elliptic pushforward
            Add:
                                λ(A) = det RpE,∗ A ⊗ OE (−χ(A) ⋅ 0E ) ∈ Pic0 (E) ≅ E.
                                                                          ​                           ​                                            ​




            This gives a tangible rigidification for wrapped sectors.

            The most important new realization is:
             Normal-ordering is not a post-processing correction. It is the finite-stage degree law

            Once the source is ΓX -graded, the corrected Gram pushforward is functorial, the raw-
            descent no-go is bypassed, and the BKM bracket comparison becomes mathematically
                                                         ​




            coherent.
            The next most important realization is:

                     Wrapped elliptic degree must be rigidified before quotienting by E.                        ​

             The Dirac operator is built from primitive normal-ordered modes, not from the sca

                                                         these seem to be recognition certifications, not algebraic

            You’re right: I blurred certificates with constructions. I’ll now separate the universal
            algebraic/homotopy-theoretic Dirac–Igusa source from the geometric K3 × E
            realization problem.

            Thought for 6m 55s
            Yes. That criticism is correct.
            What I previously called a “construction” was still mostly a recognition package: it
            specified the data whose existence would imply the Igusa/BKM/Pfaffian conclusions.
            That is not the same thing as constructing an algebraic object, a geometric
            stack/correspondence object, or a homotopy-theoretic factorization algebra.
            The draft itself already knows this distinction in several places: it calls the local
            protected observable algebra a “proposal-only schema,” says no such compact functor
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                            220/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                    Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            is constructed, and repeatedly states that the primitive comparison is a recognition
            criterion rather than an existence theorem. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…
            The right replacement is to split the project into three different objects.
                                             I. Universal algebraic Dirac–Igusa object            ​




            This can be constructed now, completely and explicitly, from the BKM denominator
            algebra plus the normal-ordered charge extension.
                                            II. Geometric pre-Hall Dirac–Igusa source                 ​




            This can also be constructed now as a derived-stack/correspondence object. It is a
            genuine geometric object, but before orientations/properness/integration it lives in a
            correspondence category, not yet as a numerical BPS theory.
                                 III. Compact protected Hall/factorization realization                    ​




            This is the real geometric theorem still to prove: that the geometric pre-Hall source
            admits orientations, finite HN completion, quotient integration, hybrid wrapped
            factorization, and primitive comparison with the universal algebraic object.
            So the actual construction should be:
                                                             Dgeom
                                                              X  ​ ⟶ Dalg
                                                                      Δ5          ​
                                                                                      ​




            where the left side is a geometric/homotopy source and the right side is the universal
            algebraic target.
            The recognition theorem is only the statement that this map is an equivalence after
            taking protected primitives and the radical quotient.

            1. The universal algebraic construction
            This is the first object you can construct completely.
            It is not a certificate. It is an honest algebraic/homotopy-theoretic object.
            1.1 The physical and Gram lattices
            Let

30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                  Attack Whitepaper Analysis
                                                  phys

            For

            1.2 A monoidal physical lift
            To construct an algebraic object graded by ΓX , choose any group homomorphism                 ​




                                                                     L : Γgram → Γphys
                                                                                  X .          ​                              ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                    222/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                     Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            This is a linear physical lift of Gram degrees. It is not required to satisfy
                                                                         ΠX (Lγ) = γ,     ​




            because that would be impossible globally: ΠX is quadratic.                                         ​




            Now define
                                                                        sL : Γgram → ΓX
                                                                          ​                             ​                   ​




            by
                                                        sL (γ) = (Lγ, ΠX (Lγ) − γ ) .
                                                              ​                                                     ​                       ​




            Then
                                                                        ΠX (sL (γ)) = γ.
                                                                                  ​               ​




            More importantly,
                                                             sL (γ + η) = sL (γ) ⋆ sL (η).
                                                                  ​                                         ​                   ​       ​




            Proof:
                    sL (γ) ⋆ sL (η) = (Lγ + Lη, ΠX (Lγ) − γ + ΠX (Lη) − η + B(Lγ, Lη)) .
                       ​             ​                                                ​                                             ​




            Since L is linear,
                                                                      L(γ + η) = Lγ + Lη.

            And by the polarization identity,
                                    ΠX (Lγ + Lη) = ΠX (Lγ) + ΠX (Lη) + B(Lγ, Lη).
                                             ​                                ​                                         ​




            Therefore
                      sL (γ) ⋆ sL (η) = (L(γ + η), ΠX (L(γ + η)) − (γ + η)) = sL (γ + η).
                           ​             ​                                                    ​                                                 ​




            So sL is a group homomorphism.
            This is the missing algebraic insight: normal ordering turns an arbitrary linear physical
            lift into a monoidal lift.

            5. The geometric construction: the pre-Hall source
            Now build the geometric object. This is different from the recognition theorem.
            Let

30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                   Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                                                           PerfX                  ​




            be the derived stack of perfect complexes on X = S × E . For a physical charge
                                                                      c ∈ Γphys

            let

            be the open-and-closed derived substack of objects with Mukai charge c.
            Because X is Calabi–Yau threefold, Mc carries a (−1)-shifted symplectic structure in
            the PTVV sense. The current draft already invokes this as the proposal-only local source
                                                                       ​




            datum. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…
            Now normal-order the stack labels.
            For
                                                               c = (c, T ) ∈ ΓX ,                             ​




            define
                                                              Mc = Mc × {T }.
                                                                  ​                   ​                           ​




            The T -factor is a discrete label. It records the normal-ordering counterterm.
            For two labels
                                                         c = (c, T ),                     c′ = (c′ , T ′ ),

            define the extension stack
                                                                               Ec, c′     ​   ​




            whose S -points are exact triangles
                                                                A′ → A → A′′

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                228/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                   Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                                             p c , c ′ : E c , c ′ → Mc × Mc ′ .

            The target map is
                                                                   q c , c ′ : E c , c ′ → Mc ⋆ c ′ .
                                                                         ​                         ​                           ​




            This is the key point:
                                                   c ⋆ c′ = (c + c′ , T + T ′ + B(c, c′ )).

            So the geometric Hall correspondence itself lands in the normal-ordered target label.
            Thus the B -correction is not a later certificate. It is built into the target of the extension
            correspondence.
            This gives an honest geometric correspondence object:
                                                         Mc × Mc ′ ← E c , c ′ → Mc ⋆ c ′ .
                                                               ​              ​                                        ​                   ​   ​




            Triple flags are defined as stacks of filtrations
                                                              0 ⊂ A3 ⊂ A23 ⊂ A123 ​                                                ​




            with successive quotients of charges
                                                                             c3 , c2 , c1 .
                                                                                          ​            ​           ​




            Associativity of the target labels follows from the cocycle identity for B .
            Therefore the pre-Hall object
                                                                                                  Hpre
                                                                                                   X       ​   ​




            is an associative algebra object in the correspondence category of (−1)-shifted
            symplectic derived stacks graded by ΓX .                                  ​




            This is a genuine geometric construction.
            What it does not yet give is a protected numerical Hall algebra. For that one still needs
            orientations, vanishing-cycle coefficients, finite-type truncation, and proper
            pushforwards.

            6. Finite HN truncation as construction
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                         229/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                          Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            Choose a Bridgeland/HN stability datum σ, a strict sector S , and a height bound R.
            Let
                                                                                   Fσ,S (R)​




            be the finite set of semistable physical charges in the sector of height at most R.
            Define
                       ΓX,R = {(c1 , 0) ⋆ ⋯ ⋆ (ck , 0) : ci ∈ Fσ,S (R),
                                ​             ​                       ​                        ​                         ​                           ∑ h S (c i ) ≤ R } .
                                                                                                                                                               ​   ​   ​    ​




                                                                                                                                                      i


            This is finite under the standard support-property and boundedness hypotheses.
            Now define

            applying the integration functor to an already constructed pre-Hall correspondence
            object.

            13. Why this is stronger than the old certificate
            The old formulation said:
                               If a compact Igusa datum exists, then compare it to gΔ5 .                  ​
                                                                                                              ​




            The new formulation says:
             1. Build a universal algebraic Dirac–Igusa object explicitly.
             2. Build a geometric normal-ordered pre-Hall correspondence object explicitly.
             3. Identify the exact functor needed to pass from geometry to protected algebra.
             4. State the comparison as a map from the geometric source to the universal algebraic
                model.
            That is the right mathematical architecture.
            The obstruction is no longer “we need an object.” We have two objects:
                                                                  Dalg
                                                                   Δ5 , L  ​
                                                                                   ​




            and
                                                                    pre
                                                                  HX .         ​




            The remaining problem is the existence and properties of the integration/realization
            functor
                                                                   IX .​




            That is much sharper.

            2. Why E appears in the K3 × E realization
            The elliptic curve is forced once the target is not merely formal, but is meant to be the
            one-dimensional chiral shadow of a compact Calabi–Yau threefold theory on
                                                                           X = S × E.

            There are four reasons.
            2.1 K3 × C is Calabi–Yau threefold only when C has trivial
            canonical bundle
            For a smooth projective curve C ,
                                                              KS×C = p∗S KS ⊗ p∗C KC .
                                                                       ​                ​       ​                   ​   ​




            Since KS ≅ OS for K3,
                           ​          ​




                                                                  KS×C ≅ p∗C KC .
                                                                                ​                   ​           ​




            Thus
                                               S × C is Calabi–Yau ⟺ KC ≅ OC .                                              ​   ​




            For a smooth projective connected curve, this means C has genus one. To get the
            translation action and quotient used in OP/DT, one chooses a base point, so C is an
            elliptic curve E .
            For g(C) = 1, the moduli of B-branes on K3 × C is not the same CY3 (−1)-shifted
            symplectic problem. The orientation/Pfaffian/DT story changes.
            So:
                         formal chiral target: any C;                                         compact CY3 source: C = E.            ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                              242/496
            2.2 The reduced OP/DT scalar theory uses the translation action of
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                      Attack Whitepaper Analysis




            E
            The scalar branch is not just a curve-general statement. It is a reduced quotient theory
            because the elliptic curve acts by translation on
                                                                   X = S × E.

            Your draft explicitly uses the quotient-by-E Behrend-weighted integration and says this
            scalar integration is not a Hall product, CoHA integration map, or orientation character.
                    raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            There is no analogous canonical translation quotient for a general curve C . For g(C) >
            1, C has no transitive algebraic group action by itself. For C = P1 , it is not CY. For a
            genus-one curve without a chosen origin, one lacks a canonical group structure until an
            origin is chosen.
            So the OP/DT scalar square forces E , not an arbitrary curve.
            2.3 The s-variable is elliptic degree
            The Borcherds variable
                                                                      sm

            is not a generic curve-degree marker in the physical realization. Under the OP/Borcherds
            dictionary,
                                                                   m = d − 1,

            where d is the degree along the elliptic curve direction. Your draft states that positive
            elliptic-degree sectors project onto all of E , so naive support-local factorization on
            Ran(E) sees only projection-finite sectors and misses the positive s-direction.
                    raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            That obstruction is specifically about the projection
                                                             pE : K3 × E → E.
                                                               ​




            If one replaced E by a general curve C , the same statement “positive C -degree
            projects onto all of C ” would remain true for irreducible C , but the physical meaning
            would not be the same, because the reduced quotient, translation symmetry, OP scalar
            theorem, and elliptic degree variable are all tied to E .
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                              243/496
            2.4 The wrapped-sector anchor uses E ≃ Pic0 (E)
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                              Attack Whitepaper Analysis




            In the improved hybrid construction, a natural wrapped-sector anchor is
                                λ(A) = det RpE,∗ A ⊗ OE (−χ(A) ⋅ 0E ) ∈ Pic0 (E) ≅ E.
                                                             ​             ​                     ​




            This identification is elliptic-specific.
            For a general curve C ,
                                                                 Pic0 (C) = J(C),

            the Jacobian. One could define an anchor in J(C), but then the wrapped sector is
            anchored in the Jacobian, not in the same curve on which the chiral factorization lives:
                                                λ(A) ∈ J(C),                   not generally in C.

            Only for genus one do we have
                                                                         J(E) ≅ E.

            So the hybrid local/wrapped factorization architecture is geometrically natural on E . For
            a general curve it would become a different theory: local insertions live on Ran(C), but
            wrapped anchors live in J(C). That is a valid possible generalization, but not the
            K3 × E Igusa realization.



            3. The corrected architecture
            The paper should separate the following two constructions.
            A. Curve-universal algebraic target
            For every smooth curve C ,
                                                        Denalg      ch
                                                           Δ5 ,C = UC (CurC (gΔ5 )) .
                                                                 ​
                                                                     ​          ​          ​


                                                                                                     ​
                                                                                                         ​   ​




            This is formal. It depends on C only as a carrier for chiral operations.
            It does not claim a compact K3 × C physical source.
            It does not by itself produce OP/DT invariants.
            It does not know about elliptic translation quotienting.

            It does not solve the wrapped-degree locality problem.
            B. Elliptic physical realization target

            one specializes

            and asks for a source-side object

            such that
                                                                           alg

                                                                     X,∗ FX ) ≅ gΔ5 .                           ​                   ​


                                                                                                                                                            ​

            This is the K3 × E compact realization problem.

              is the formal target for the K3-integrated chiral shadow of a compact source on

            to avoid implying that the compact K3 × E source has already been built.

            I chose E because the physical problem is K3 × E , and your draft’s compact
            realization is explicitly a K3-to-E specialization whose locality obstruction and OP/DT
            scalar square are elliptic. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            1. The normal-ordered charge group
            Define the polarization defect
                                            B(c, c′ ) = (Q ⋅ Q′ , Q ⋅ P ′ + Q′ ⋅ P , P ⋅ P ′ )                                                ​




            for
                                                        c = (Q, P ),                          c′ = (Q′ , P ′ ).

            Then
                                             ΠX (c + c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ) + B(c, c′ ).
                                                   ​                          ​                           ​                               ​




            Also
                                                                  B(c, c) = 2ΠX (c).                  ​               ​




            This identity is pure lattice polarization. It is independent of orientation choices, parity
            conventions, Bott signs, or physical Hilbert-space lifts.
            Now define the normal-ordered charge group
                                                                 ΓX = Γphys
                                                                    ​




                                                                       X    ⊕B Γgram .​           ​                       ​   ​




            As a set,
                                                                 ΓX = Γphys
                                                                       X​   ⊕ Γgram .     ​                       ​




            Write elements as
                                                                            c = (c, T ).

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                        249/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            Define multiplication
                                            (c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ ) = (c + c′ , T + T ′ + B(c, c′ )).                                      ​




            The identity is
                                                                                      (0, 0),

            and the inverse is
                                                        (c, T )−1 = (−c, −T + B(c, c)).

            Because B is bilinear,
                                                      B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c, c′′ ) + B(c′ , c′′ ),

            so ⋆ is associative. Since B is symmetric, ⋆ is commutative.
            Define the normal-ordered Gram map
                                           ΠX : ΓX → Γgram ,
                                              ​          ​                    ​                  ΠX (c, T ) = ΠX (c) − T .
                                                                                                         ​                       ​           ​




            Then
                                                             ΠX (c ⋆ c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ).
                                                                ​                                    ​                       ​       ​




            Proof:
                                 ΠX ((c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ )) = ΠX (c + c′ ) − T − T ′ − B(c, c′ )
                                       ​                                                     ​




                                      = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ) + B(c, c′ ) − T − T ′ − B(c, c′ )
                                                  ​                     ​




                                                       = (ΠX (c) − T ) + (ΠX (c′ ) − T ′ ).
                                                                    ​                                            ​




            So the basic degree object is the abelian group
                                                                                      (ΓX , ⋆)   ​           ​




            together with the homomorphism
                                                                            ΠX : ΓX → Γgram .
                                                                                  ​      ​                           ​   ​




            This is the replacement for raw Π-descent.

            10. Orientation gerbe: construct the twisted object instead
            of assuming triviality
            This is the main anti-certificate move.
            Do not assume the orientation line exists globally. Construct the orientation gerbe.
            For each R, c, let
                                                                              KR,c        ​




            be the virtual canonical/determinant line of the d-critical stack MR,c .                                                                         ​




            Define the square-root gerbe
                                                                   OrR,c =    ​                   KR,c                 ​ ​       ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                                       262/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                               Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            as the μ2-gerbe whose objects over U → MR,c are pairs
                          ​                                                                                          ​




                                                                                                                         ∼
                                                      (L, ϕ),                            ϕ : L⊗2                                         ​       KR,c ∣U .           ​           ​




            This gerbe always exists as a stack, even when it has no global section.
            Let
                                                             πR,c : OrR,c → MR,c

            be the projection.
            On OrR,c there is a tautological line

            with
                                                                    1/2
                                                             (LR,c )⊗2 = πR,c

                                                                    Mor

            The extension correspondence lifts to the fibre product gerbe
                                  Eor

                                         ​               ​

                                                                                                                                                     ​                   ​

                                                                                                                                                                                             ​
                                                                                                                                                                                                 ​       ​   ​




            The Joyce-style multiplicativity becomes a morphism of gerbes

            If it is not strictly trivial, the theory is twisted by the corresponding μ2-gerbe class. That
            is still a constructed object.
            Therefore the correct object is not an oriented Hall algebra first. It is a μ2-twisted Hall
            correspondence object:
                                                                                                                                                                                                     ​




                                                         Htw      or     or        or
                                                          X,R = (MR,c , ER,c,c′ , FR ) .

            A choice of global orientation is a section of this gerbe. The compact scalar/Pfaffian
            comparison needs such a trivialization, but the twisted object itself is constructed
            without it.

            This is the cleanest way to avoid certificate cheating.

            12. The quotient by E as stack, not assumption
            Do not require the E -quotient to be free. Use the quotient stack.
            For every finite stage,
                                                                 Mor          or
                                                                  R,c /E := [MR,c /E].
                                                                            ​                                      ​                    ​




            This automatically remembers stabilizers.
            Finite-stabilizer characters are not extra assumptions at the construction level. They are
            inertia data of the quotient stack. If one wants a scalar OP-like quotient, one later
            projects to the trivial stabilizer-character sector.
            So the stack-level construction is:

            The E[2], E[N ], and H 1(BE[N ]; F2) issues are now encoded as orbifold sectors of
            this quotient stack. Trivialization is a later scalarization condition, not a prerequisite for

            constructing the twisted Hall object.

            13. Hybrid local/wrapped construction
            Now construct the hybrid factorization object geometrically.
            Let
                                                                     pE : X → E.
                                                                          ​




            Define two classes of objects.
            Projection-finite:
                                            A ∈ Mloc ⟺ pE (Supp A) ⊂ E is finite.
                                                                               ​




            Wrapped:
                                            A ∈ Mwr
                                                 b  ⟺ (pE )∗ [A]1 = b[E],
                                                             ​                     ​   ​      ​                     b > 0.

            A positive elliptic-degree one-dimensional support projects onto all of E , so ordinary
            Ran(E)-locality sees only projection-finite sectors. The draft already emphasizes that
            the full s-direction needs finite-stage local/wrapped correspondence data before
            quotienting by E . raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            18. Algorithms
            Algorithm 1: Build ΓX                                                                                    ​




            Input:
                                                                                                                                                                  Γphys
                                                                                                                                                                   X ,        ​                ΠX .         ​




            Steps:
             1. Compute B(c, c′ ) = ΠX (c + c′ ) − ΠX (c) − ΠX (c′ ).
             2. Form ΓX = Γphys      ⊕ Γgram .
                                                                                                                                     ​                                                     ​                               ​




                               X
             3. Define ⋆ using B .
                                    ​                                                   ​                                                        ​




             4. Define ΠX (c, T ) = ΠX (c) − T .
             5. Verify associativity and additivity.
                                                        ​                                                                        ​




            Output:
                                                                                                                                                                      (ΓX , ⋆, ΠX ).
                                                                                                                                                                          ​                             ​




            Algorithm 2: Build the algebraic target
            Input:
                                                                                                                                                                                                                phys
                                                                                                                    gΔ5 ,                ​
                                                                                                                                             ​           L0 : Γgram → ΓX ,
                                                                                                                                                                  ​                    ​                               ​       C.

            Steps:
             1. Define sL (γ) = (L0γ , ΠX (L0γ ) − γ).
             2. Regrade gΔ by sL .
                                        0
                                                ​                                                               ​                                    ​    ​


                                            ​




             3. Define gΔ ,L .

                                        5                       0
