            Algorithm 3: Build the geometric pre-Hall source
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                    Attack Whitepaper Analysis




            Input:
                                                                      X = S × E,                            ΓX .
                                                                                                               ​




            Steps:
             1. Construct Perf cpt (X).
             2. Decompose by physical charge c.
             3. Add normal-ordering labels T : Mc = Mc × {T }.

            21. The decisive new point
            The object is no longer a certificate.
            The certificate was:

            II. The central conceptual repair
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                 Attack Whitepaper Analysis




            The paper should stop saying, even implicitly:
                                                     “Construct DX from a certificate.”
                                                                     ​




            Instead say:
            “Construct a twisted source object canonically; then ask whether its primitive quot

            That is the difference between a recognition problem and a construction.
            The latest draft already has the finite-source obstruction certificate and notes that D0,
            O1, O1+ , O2, source Koszul data, hybrid wrapped factorization, and primitive
            recognition are certificate data. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc… But to go beyond this, build
            the orientation-gerbe-twisted object first. A global orientation trivialization should not
            be required to define the source; it should only be required to descend the twisted
            object to an ordinary Pfaffian theory.
            So the new hierarchy should be:
                                                      Hpre   exists as correspondences;

                                                Htw      exists over the orientation gerbe;

                                          Hor    exists only after orientation trivialization;

                      HDI        is the Dirac–Igusa realization if primitive comparison holds.

            IV. The biggest remaining vulnerabilities
            1. The paper is still too certificate-forward
            The latest text repeatedly says “certificate,” “supplied,” “conditional,” and “open.” That is
            honest, but after enough repetition it becomes exhausting. The cure is not to overclaim.
            The cure is to construct the pre-objects:
                                                              pre                                alg
                                                             HX ,   ​    Htw
                                                                          X,   ​               DΔ5 ,C,L .
                                                                                                   ​
                                                                                                           ​




            Then the certificates become trivialization/comparison data, not the core architecture.
            2. The word “physical” remains slightly unstable
            The draft now uses Γform
                                 X   and warns that earlier Γphys   is formal full-Mukai bookkeeping,
            not the full 4d N = 4 electric–magnetic lattice. That is good. Keep this distinction
                                                    ​




                                                               X                                       ​




            everywhere. The abstract still risks saying “physical charge lattice” too early. Use:
                                                                           Γform
                                                                            X      ​




            for the formal dyonic Mukai lattice;
                                                                           alg/eff
                                                                          ΓX               ​




            for effective Hall support;
                                                                             =4
                                                                          ΓN
                                                                           X           ​




            only if discussing the full physical compactification lattice.
            3. E3 remains underdefined
            The paper still says “holomorphic E3-factorization algebra.” That phrase needs a
            precise model:
                                                                        Facthol (X)

            first;
            then a local E3-shadow after formality/framing.
                                    ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                     283/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                            Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            The finite compact E3-source obstruction certificate already lists formality point,
            framing, QME, anomaly, HN, specialization, hybrid residual, and transition compatibility.
                                               ​




                raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc… The next upgrade is to define the source category and
            operad before listing obstructions.
            4. The orientation section is excellent but too heavy
            The latest O1, O1+, and O2 material is sophisticated and much improved. It correctly
            separates degree-two gerbes, degree-one finite-stabilizer characters, higher even N ,
            Weyl projective cocycles, quotient torsors, and Pfaffian local normal forms.
                    raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            But it should be split:
                 main text: 2-page theorem statement;
                 appendix: full obstruction algebra.
            The great-paper version is readable first, auditable second.
            5. The scalar square should be pushed later
            The scalar OP/DT branch is important, but it is not central to the new mathematics. The
            paper already says it is orientation-forgetful and imported. Keep it as a consistency
            check, not as a driver.
            6. The eight-row section may dilute the main breakthrough
            The eight-row certificates are valuable but likely belong in a companion paper unless
            they directly motivate the N = 1 construction. The main paper’s breakthrough is
            normal-ordered Dirac–Igusa structure, not the row atlas.

            VI. Insertable new section: “Constructed objects, not

            certificates”
            I would add this after the introduction.
            Constructed objects
            The compact realization problem is not one object but a chain of objects.
            First, the algebraic object is constructed for every smooth curve C :
                                        Δ5 ,C,L = (gΔ5 ,L , DenΔ5 ,C,L , DΔ5 , Pf
                                       Dalg                                           = Δ5 ) .
                                                               alg        alg     alg
                                                    ​              ​             ​                                   ​                     ​                               ​

                                                                           ​

                                              ​                                                      ​                                 ​




            Second, the geometric pre-Hall object is constructed as a correspondence object:
                                                             Hpre
                                                              X = ( Mc , E c , c , F c , c , c ) ,
                                                                       ​
                                                                                ′         ′ ′′   ​               ​                                 ​




            with
                                                                       Mc := Mc × {T },
                                                                                     ​                   ​




            and
                                                                               q : E c , c ′ → Mc ⋆ c ′ .
                                                                                             ​                                     ​




            Third, the twisted protected object is constructed over the orientation gerbe:
                                                                                                                         pre
                                                                               Htw   tw
                                                                                X = I (HX ).
                                                                                         ​                                     ​




            A global orientation trivialization is not required to define HtwX . It is required to descend
            to an ordinary untwisted Pfaffian theory.
                                                                                                                                                           ​




            The compact Dirac–Igusa realization theorem is then the comparison:
                                                                                                                                               ?
                                                        ΠX,∗ H 0 Prim(Htw
                                                               ​


                                                                       X )/ Rad ≅ gΔ5 .                      ​                                         ​
                                                                                                                                                               ​
                                                                                                                                                                   ​




            VII. The hard next theorem
            The next major theorem should not be “Pf = Δ5.” That follows only after recognition.                         ​




            The hard next theorem is:
                                                                                                                                                           pre
             The normal-ordered derived stack correspondence HX exists and is associative.                                                                             ​         ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                                                     296/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                       Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            This is approachable and foundational.
            State it as:
               Let Mc be the derived moduli stack of compactly supported perfect complexes on
               X = S × E of formal Mukai charge c. Let c = (c, T ). Then the stacks Mc =
                             ​




               Mc × {T }, together with exact-triangle extension stacks and target maps to Mc⋆c ,
                                                                                                                   ​




                                                                                                                       ′

               define an associative ΓX -graded Hall object in derived correspondences.
                       ​                                                                                                   ​




                                                        ​




            This theorem is truly constructive and it removes a major amount of certificate
            language.

            VIII. Final assessment
            The latest draft has become intellectually serious. It has the right caution, the right
            automorphic normalization, the right physical locality obstruction, and the right normal-
            ordered descent mechanism. Its biggest remaining weakness is not mathematical
            ambition; it is that the compact side still reads too much like a list of certificates.
            To take it “beyond the platonic ideal,” make the compact side into a constructed
            hierarchy:
                                                      derived correspondence source                    ​




                                           orientation-gerbe twisted protected source                          ​




                                           hybrid local/wrapped factorization source

                                                             source chiral coalgebra               ​




                                                primitive normal-ordered Lie algebra                       ​




                                                               comparison to gΔ5 .     ​
                                                                                           ​   ​




            Then the paper becomes more than a beautiful normalization ledger. It becomes a new
            blueprint for how a Dirac/Pfaffian first-order object should emerge from compact
            Calabi–Yau Hall geometry, with the Igusa square root as its automorphic shadow.

            I’ll make this self-contained and construction-first: algebraic Dirac–Igusa object,
            geometric Hall correspondence source, twisted/oriented protected Hall object, then only
            afterward the comparison theorems.
            The new refinement is to stop requiring orientation or compactness before defining the
            source: build pre-Hall correspondences first, then twist by the orientation gerbe.
            Thought for 7m 12s
            According to a document from April 29, 2026, the latest manuscript already knows the
            crucial distinction: the K0-Borcherds determinant DX = Δ5, the Gritsenko–Nikulin
            denominator algebra, and the OP scalar square are not compact source constructions;
                                                     ​                                   ​            ​




            the missing object is a finite-first compact Hall/factorization realization with normal-
            ordered Gram descent, hybrid local/wrapped sectors, source coalgebra, and primitive
            comparison. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc… The draft also explicitly states that the
            compact source is not reconstructed from the Borcherds product, scalar square, target
            current envelope, target bar–cobar counit, or orientation lines alone.
                    raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            Here is the Etingof–Kac-style reconstruction: define the algebra, its grading, its
            generators and relations, its geometric source, its correspondence laws, its orientation-
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                          298/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                 Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            gerbe twist, its finite algorithms, and only then the comparison theorem. No certificate is
            allowed to substitute for an object.

            0. The non-negotiable split
            There are four different mathematical objects.
                                                              Dalg
                                                               Δ5 ,C,L
                                                                  ​
                                                                                      ​   ​




            is the curve-universal algebraic Dirac–Igusa object. It is constructed directly from the
            Gritsenko–Nikulin BKM algebra and the normal-ordered charge extension.
                                                                Hpre
                                                                 X,Γ
                                                                          ​       ​




            is the geometric normal-ordered Hall correspondence source. It is constructed from
            derived moduli stacks of compact objects and exact-triangle correspondences, before
            orientation or integration.
                                                                Htw
                                                                 X,Γ
                                                                          ​       ​




            is the orientation-gerbe-twisted protected Hall object. It is constructed over the
            square-root gerbe of the determinant line; it does not require a global orientation
            trivialization.
                                                                DDI
                                                                 X    ​       ​




            is the compact Dirac–Igusa realization, obtained only after the twisted source has a
            normal-ordered primitive algebra and a source-to-target comparison.
            The comparison
                                                                                              ?
                                                ΠX,∗ H 0 Primprot (Htw
                                                         ​




                                                                    X,Γ
                                                                        ) / Rad ≅ gΔ5     ​       ​

                                                                                                      ​
                                                                                                          ​




            is a theorem to prove about constructed objects. It is not part of their definition.

            1. Formal charge data
            Let

                                                                   X = S × E,

            where S is a smooth K3 surface and E is a smooth elliptic curve.
            Let
                                    ΛS = H (S , Z) = H 0 (S, Z) ⊕ H 2 (S, Z) ⊕ H 4 (S, Z)

            with Mukai pairing
                                             (r, D, s) ⋅ (r′ , D′ , s′ ) = D ⋅ D′ − rs′ − r′ s.

            Define the formal dyonic Mukai lattice
                                                                 Γform
                                                                  X   ​= ΛS ⊕ ΛS .       ​               ​   ​




            A charge is
                                                                    c = (Q, P ).

            Define the Gram-index lattice
                                                                    Γgram = Z3 .
                                                                              ​                      ​




            Define the raw Gram map
                                                                     Q2         P2
                                                       ΠX (Q, P ) = ( , Q ⋅ P ,    ).
                                                                     2          2
                                                             ​                       ​                               ​   ​




            This is quadratic, not additive.
            For c = (Q, P ) and c′ = (Q′ , P ′ ), define
                                             B(c, c′ ) = (Q ⋅ Q′ , Q ⋅ P ′ + Q′ ⋅ P , P ⋅ P ′ ) .                                ​




            Then
                                             ΠX (c + c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ) + B(c, c′ ).
                                                   ​                      ​                      ​                           ​




            Also
                                                                 B(c, c) = 2ΠX (c).          ​                   ​




            This is the polarization identity for the quadratic Gram map.

            2. Normal-ordered charge group
            Define
                                                                  ΓX = Γform
                                                                     ​




                                                                        X    ⊕B Γgram .   ​               ​       ​       ​




            As a set,
                                                                  ΓX = Γform
                                                                        X​   ⊕ Γgram .        ​               ​




            Write elements as
                                                                                 c = (c, T ).

            Define the product
                                            (c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ ) = (c + c′ , T + T ′ + B(c, c′ )).                                       ​




            The identity is
                                                                                   (0, 0),

            and the inverse is
                                                      (c, T )−1 = (−c, −T + B(c, c)).                                                 ​




            Associativity follows because
                                                  B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c, c′′ ) + B(c′ , c′′ ).

            Commutativity follows because B is symmetric.
            Define the normal-ordered Gram map
                                           ΠX : ΓX → Γgram ,
                                              ​        ​                     ​            ΠX (c, T ) = ΠX (c) − T .
                                                                                                      ​                       ​               ​




            Then
                                                           ΠX (c ⋆ c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ).
                                                              ​                                   ​                   ​           ​




            Proof:
                                 ΠX ((c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ )) = ΠX (c + c′ ) − T − T ′ − B(c, c′ )
                                       ​                                              ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                        301/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                              Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                      = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c ) + B(c, c′ ) − T − T ′ − B(c, c′ )
                                                  ​                       ​
                                                                              ′


                                                          = (ΠX (c) − T ) + (ΠX (c′ ) − T ′ ).
                                                                      ​                                                     ​




            Thus ΓX is the correct additive source degree group.
                             ​




            3. Flag normal-ordering
            For c1, … , ck ∈ Γform
                     ​




                              X , ​               ​




                                        (c1 , 0) ⋆ ⋯ ⋆ (ck , 0) = (∑ ci , ∑ B(ci , cj )) .
                                             ​                                    ​                                     ​               ​             ​               ​       ​       ​




                                                                                                        i                                       i<j


            Therefore
                                                 ΠX ((c1 , 0) ⋆ ⋯ ⋆ (ck , 0)) = ∑ ΠX (ci ).
                                                      ​       ​                                 ​                                                             ​   ​       ​       ​




                                                                                                                                                 i


            Interpretation: the raw bound-state charge is ∑i ci , but its raw Gram matrix contains
            cross-terms. The normal-ordered degree subtracts precisely the pairwise interaction
                                                                                                                    ​           ​




            counterterm.

            4. Raw-descent no-go
            Let
                                                                                      P = ⨁ Pc                  ​                   ​




                                                                                           c∈Γform
                                                                                              X
                                                                                                            ​




            be a physical-charge-graded Lie superalgebra with
                                                                              [Pc , Pc′ ] ⊆ Pc+c′ .
                                                                                       ​   ​                                                ​




            Raw ΠX -descent would require
                         ​




                                                           ΠX (c + c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ )
                                                                  ​                                 ​                                                     ​




            for every nonzero bracket, hence
                                                                                      B(c, c′ ) = 0.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                                                                302/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                                         Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            But the real-root strings of gΔ require, for distinct real simple generators ei , ej ,
                                                                      5   ​
                                                                              ​                                                                                               ​       ​




                                                                                      [ei , ej ] =
                                                                                                  0,
                                                                                                  ​                       ​




                                                                                   [ei , [ei , ej ]] =
                                                                                          ​           0.         ​               ​




            If ci , cj lift their degrees, the first bracket forces
                    ​   ​




                                                                                    B(ci , cj ) = 0.      ​                   ​




            The second bracket forces
                                                                                  B(ci , ci + cj ) = 0.
                                                                                              ​                   ​                   ​




            But
                                   B(ci , ci + cj ) = B(ci , ci ) + B(ci , cj ) = 2ΠX (ci ) =
                                           ​         ​            ​                   ​      0.      ​                                               ​       ​   ​   ​




            Contradiction.
            Therefore:
                               raw Gram descent cannot realize the full BKM bracket.                                                                                              ​




            The draft already identifies this as a proved raw-descent obstruction and presents
            normal-ordering as the structural repair. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            12. Orientation gerbe instead of orientation assumption
            Now remove the certificate cheat.
            Do not assume a global orientation line.
            Construct the orientation gerbe.
            For each finite stack MR,c , let          ​

                                                                    OrR,c =      ​                     KR,c .      ​ ​        ​

            14. Product, coproduct, primitives
            For the oriented correspondence
                                                Mor     or      or        or

            define

            Here TS is the Thom–Sebastiani map for vanishing cycles, together with the
            tautological tensor product of square-root lines on the orientation gerbe.
            Define the coproduct
                                                             ΔR = (pR )! ∘ TS−1 ∘(qR )∗ .

                                                   PRtw = ker (ΔR − id ⊗1 − 1 ⊗ id) .

                                                                 ​             ​                                                                                     ​




            satisfies
                                                                        tw     tw         tw

            15. Hybrid local/wrapped source
            The latest draft correctly notes that the full Igusa s-direction cannot be recovered from
            a naive projection-finite Ran(E)-object; it requires local/wrapped correspondences,
            rigidified wrapped prequotients with anchors, quotient-after-correspondence descent,
            and trace degree sb . raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…
                                           R   ​




            Define
                                                        Ranhyb (E) = ∐ Ran(E)[b].                                   ​




                                                                                                         b≥0

            The b = 0 stratum is projection-finite.
            The b > 0 strata are wrapped elliptic-degree sectors.
            Projection-finite objects satisfy
                                                                             pE (Supp A) ⊂ E
                                                                                 ​




            finite.
            Wrapped objects satisfy
                                                        (pE )∗ [A]1 = b[E],

            Algorithm 1: Build ΓX                       ​

                                                                                                                         ΠX .         ​

                                                                                  X  ​                                    ​                                             ​

            Output:
                                                                                 (ΓX , ⋆, ΠX ).  ​                            ​

        105. Never call a comparison datum a construction.
        106. Never call a determinant identity a Hilbert-space construction.
        107. Never call the target current envelope a compact source.
        108. Never define CX from the target.
        109. Never quotient by E before wrapped correspondences.

        110. Never use Γgram as the raw Hall charge lattice.

            Use this as the opening of the compact-realization part:
              The compact object is not defined by a recognition certificate. We construct first the
              normal-ordered Hall correspondence source. The degree group is ΓX , not Γgram .
              The moduli stack in degree c = (c, T ) is Mc = Mc × {T }, and an exact triangle
                                                                                                                    ​                   ​




              with charges c, c′ lands in degree c ⋆ c′ . Thus the Gram correction B(c, c′ ) is built
                                                                                    ​




              into the geometric correspondence target, rather than applied afterward.
              We then construct the orientation-gerbe-twisted protected Hall object over Kc . A
              global orientation is a trivialization problem for this gerbe, not a prerequisite for
                                                                                                                                            ​




              defining the twisted source. Finally we construct the hybrid local/wrapped
              factorization source over E , the source chiral bar coalgebra, the source Dirac
              operator, and the source Pfaffian. Only after these objects exist do we state the
              comparison with the universal algebraic Dirac–Igusa object built from gΔ .                                5   ​
                                                                                                                                ​




            23. The decisive conceptual upgrade
            The old architecture was:
                                                       certificate ⇒ compact object.

            The new architecture is:
             pre-Hall correspondences ⇒ orientation-gerbe twisted Hall object ⇒ hybrid factor

            That is the Etingof–Kac style: construct the algebra and the category first; then prove its
            presentation, PBW theorem, radical quotient, and character.
            The compact Dirac–Igusa object is therefore:

            1. Formal dyonic Mukai lattice

                                                                       X = S × E,

                                                       c = (Q, P ),                    c′ = (Q′ , P ′ ),

            Then
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                              332/496

                                                                   B(c, c) = 2ΠX (c).              ​           ​




            This is the fundamental lattice identity.

            2. Normal-ordered charge group
            Define
                                                                ΓX = Γform
                                                                     ​




                                                                      X    ⊕B Γgram .  ​       ​                   ​   ​




            As a set,
                                                                 ΓX = Γform
                                                                       X ​  ⊕ Γgram .      ​               ​




            Write elements as
                                                                             c = (c, T ).

            Define multiplication
                                          (c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ ) = (c + c′ , T + T ′ + B(c, c′ )).                                  ​




            The identity is
                                                                                   (0, 0).

            The inverse is
                                                       (c, T )−1 = (−c, −T + B(c, c)).                                     ​




            Associativity follows from bilinearity:
                                                  B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c, c′′ ) + B(c′ , c′′ ).

            Indeed,
                                                             ((c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ )) ⋆ (c′′ , T ′′ )

            has second component

                                     B(c, c′ ) + B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c′ , c′′ ) + B(c, c′ + c′′ ).

            Since B is symmetric, ΓX is abelian.      ​




            Define the normal-ordered Gram homomorphism

            algebra; it is the source in derived correspondences.

30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                       Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            The source map is
                                                             p c , c ′ : E c , c ′ → Mc × Mc ′ .

            is an associative algebra object in the correspondence category of derived stacks.
            This is a genuine construction. It is not a certificate.

            15. Hybrid local/wrapped source
            The projection
                                                                                       pE : X → E  ​




            forces a hybrid construction.
            A sector is projection-finite if
                                                                              pE (Supp A) ⊂ E
                                                                                       ​




            is finite.
            A one-dimensional sector is wrapped over E if
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                                                                  352/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                                Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                                                   (pE )∗ [A]1 = b[E],
                                                                         ​       ​           ​                                                 b > 0.

            Positive elliptic degree implies the support projects onto all of E , so a naive Ran(E)-
            local object misses the positive s-direction. The manuscript already names this as the
            Ran(E)-support-locality obstruction and says the repair requires local/wrapped
            correspondences before the reduced E -quotient. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…
            Define the hybrid Ran base
                                                                   Ranhyb (E) = ∐ Ran(E)[b].                           ​                                    ​




                                                                                                                 b≥0

            20. The Igusa equality theorem
            Now the equality is a theorem with an actual proof.
            Theorem
            Assume the comparison morphism
                                                                                          ΦDI
                                                                                           X               ​




            induces an isomorphism
                                                               ΠX,∗ PXtw / Rad ≅ gΔ5
                                                                             ​        ​                                                             ​




            and identifies source parity with the Gritsenko–Nikulin parity. Then

                                                                                    X = Δ5 .

            Proof
            By construction,

            2. Formal charge algebra
            Let
                                    ΛS = H (S, Z) = H 0 (S, Z) ⊕ H 2 (S, Z) ⊕ H 4 (S, Z)
                                         ​




            with Mukai pairing
                                             (r, D, s) ⋅ (r′ , D′ , s′ ) = D ⋅ D′ − rs′ − r′ s.

            Define the formal dyonic Mukai lattice
                                                                 Γform

            3. Raw/split/cochain repair
            The draft needs a notation firewall here.

            3.2 Zero-Gram splitting

            3.4 Cohomology language
            Do not write unsafe statements like “B ∈ Hsym
                                                      2
                                                          ” unless the cochain complex is defined.    ​




            The precise statement is:
                                                                    2
                                                               B ∈ Zgrp (Γform
                                                                          X ; Γgram )
                                                                               ​                          ​                       ​




            for the additive group with trivial action, because
                                     B(c, c′ ) + B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c′ , c′′ ) + B(c, c′ + c′′ ).

            Moreover,
                                                                         B = dΠX                              ​




            as a set-theoretic/quadratic group cochain:
                               dΠX (c, c′ ) = −ΠX (c) − ΠX (c′ ) + ΠX (c + c′ ) = B(c, c′ ).
                                        ​                          ​                   ​                                  ​




            It is not the coboundary of a linear homomorphism.
            That distinction matters.

            7. The universal finite Hall source
            Define the finite Hall state object at height R as the PBW truncation
                                                                   HR = U (gΔ5 )≤R .
                                                                             ​

                                                                                                                        ​
                                                                                                                            ​                   ​   ​




            Concretely,
                                                                                                     HR     ​




            is the span of PBW monomials
                                                                    xγ 1 , i 1 ⋯ xγ k , i k
                                                                                 ​           ​
                                                                                                 ​

                                                                                                                                    ​   ​
                                                                                                                                            ​




            with
                                                                                 γj ∈ A R ,
                                                                                         ​                          ​




            and
                                                                 h(γ1 + ⋯ + γk ) ≤ R.
                                                                     ​                                                          ​




            Since AR is finite and each gγ is finite-dimensional, HR is finite-dimensional.
                           ​                                 ​                                                                                              ​




            Multiplication is not internal at fixed R; it is
                                                         μR,R′ : HR ⊗ HR′ → HR+R′ .  ​                          ​                                               ​   ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                                              376/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                     Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            It is induced by multiplication in U (gΔ ).                         5   ​
                                                                                        ​




            Coproduct is
                                                        Δ R : HR →
                                                             ​          ​                         ⨁              ​   HR 1 ⊗ HR 2 ,
                                                                                                                          ​
                                                                                                                              ​

                                                                                                                                              ​
                                                                                                                                                  ​




                                                                                            R 1 +R 2 ≤R
                                                                                              ​         ​




            induced by
                                                                 Δ(x) = x ⊗ 1 + 1 ⊗ x

            on primitive generators.
            Associativity:
                                       μR+R′ ,R′′ ∘ (μR,R′ ⊗ 1) = μR,R′ +R′′ ∘ (1 ⊗ μR′ ,R′′ ).
                                                        ​                   ​                                                     ​                       ​




            Coassociativity:
                                                                 (Δ ⊗ 1)Δ = (1 ⊗ Δ)Δ.

            Compatibility:
                                                  Δμ = (μ ⊗ μ)(1 ⊗ τ ⊗ 1)(Δ ⊗ Δ).

            Thus
                                                                 HR = (HR , μR,R′ , ΔR )
                                                                    ​                             ​                                   ​   ​




            is a finite stage in a filtered Hopf tower.
            The primitive subspace is
                                               PR = {x ∈ HR : Δx = x ⊗ 1 + 1 ⊗ x}.
                                                    ​                                   ​                                                             ​




            By PBW for enveloping algebras,
                                                                                        PR = pR .
                                                                                              ​              ​        ​




            So the finite primitive Lie superalgebra is explicitly constructed.

            10. Orientation-gerbe-first construction
            The manuscript currently treats orientation mostly as a certificate. The repair is to make
            the orientation gerbe the base of the source.
            For every finite primitive degree γ , define the finite Dirac module
                                                      Kγ = Vγ ⊕ ΠVγ∨ ,
                                                                 ​      ​                 ​         Vγ = gγ .
                                                                                                         ​           ​




            It carries the canonical odd pairing
                                                                       ⟨v, Πϕ⟩ = ϕ(v).

            Define the determinant line
                                                                      Ldet,γ = Ber(Kγ ).
                                                                                ​                    ​       ​




            Define the square-root gerbe

30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                        Attack Whitepaper Analysis


                                                               OrR,γ =      ​
                                                                                                     Ldet,γ .               ​ ​               ​




            Over a test point, an object is
                                                                                                                      ∼
                                                     (M , φ),               φ : M ⊗2                                                      ​       Ldet,γ .    ​




            There is a tautological Pfaffian line
                                                                                    Ltaut
                                                                                     Pf,γ        ​    ​




            on OrR,γ satisfying

                                                               (Ltaut
                                                                 Pf,γ )
                                                                        ⊗2

            Take the μ2-anti-invariant sector on each gerbe band. This is the Pfaffian sector.
                              ​




            This constructs H tw before any global orientation choice. A global orientation is a
            section
                                                                Spec C → OrR .                                    ​




            Thus orientation is no longer a precondition for the source.
            For a geometric realization, replace the point by the finite moduli stack:
                                                                OrR,c =         ​                    KR,c ,           ​ ​




            where KR,c is the virtual canonical/determinant line. The algebraic construction shows

                                                                          ​                                 ​                                                   ​




            Relations are exactly the eight associativity words:
                           LLL, LLW , LW L, W LL, LWW , W LW , WW L, WWW .                                                                                                      ​




            Because degree addition is associative and AJR is a homomorphism, all eight diagrams
            commute strictly.
                                                                                                                                ​




            Define the universal hybrid factorization functor
                                                                      hyb
                                                             FR               ​   : HybR → sVectfd .                ​                                       ​       ​




            Set
                                                                                      hyb

            and
                                                                                  hyb
                                                                          FR (Wγ,a ) = Vγ .
                                                                                        ​                                   ​               ​




            The four binary operations are the BKM bracket/product maps, depending on whether
            we are building primitives or the enveloping Hall state.
            This is now an actual finite hybrid local/wrapped source, not a certificate.

            such that:
             1. it preserves ΓX -degrees;   ​

            At Etingof–Kazhdan–Gelfand–Kac standard, the fix is not to rephrase the missing
            compact geometry as another certificate. The fix is to construct a universal finite
            Dirac–Igusa Hall source by generators, relations, PBW, Hopf structure, normal-
            ordering, chiral bar/cobar, orientation-gerbe twist, and Pfaffian. Then the geometric
            K3 × E theorem becomes a realization theorem: construct a functor from compact
            K3 × E Hall geometry into this universal object.

            That is the only honest way to close the algebraic theorem while not falsely claiming that
            the actual compact derived K3 × E moduli geometry has already been built.

            0. The corrected theorem strategy
            The current paper’s problematic direction is:
                                          compact source certificate ⟹ gΔ5 ⟹ Δ5 .                               ​
                                                                                                                    ​       ​




            The Etingof–Kac repair is:
                                      construct a universal finite source object UDI

                                                             RX : Hgeom

                                                                           Δ5 ,E,L .        ​
                                                                                                        ​   ​




            This separates the two theorems:
                      Universal algebraic Dirac–Igusa theorem: proved by construction.                                                  ​




                    Compact K3 × E realization theorem: geometric realization problem.                                                      ​




            The universal object is not a certificate. It is the constructed object whose structure the
            compact geometry must realize.

            1. Lattice and normal-ordering data
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                    Attack Whitepaper Analysis




            Let
                                    ΛS = H (S, Z) = H 0 (S, Z) ⊕ H 2 (S, Z) ⊕ H 4 (S, Z)
                                         ​




            with Mukai pairing
                                             (r, D, s) ⋅ (r′ , D′ , s′ ) = D ⋅ D′ − rs′ − r′ s.

            Define the formal dyonic Mukai lattice
                                                                 Γform
                                                                  X    = ΛS ⊕ ΛS .
                                                                         ​                  ​               ​   ​




            An element is
                                                                       c = (Q, P ).

            Define
                                                                       Γgram = Z3 .
                                                                                 ​                  ​




            Define the raw Gram map
                                                                     Q2         P2
                                                       ΠX (Q, P ) = ( , Q ⋅ P ,    ).
                                                                     2          2
                                                             ​                          ​                           ​       ​




            Define the polarization defect
                                             B(c, c′ ) = ΠX (c + c′ ) − ΠX (c) − ΠX (c′ ).
                                                                   ​                                    ​               ​       ​




            For
                                                        c = (Q, P ),                    c′ = (Q′ , P ′ ),

            this is
                                             B(c, c′ ) = (Q ⋅ Q′ , Q ⋅ P ′ + Q′ ⋅ P , P ⋅ P ′ ) .                                   ​




            Hence
                                             ΠX (c + c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ) + B(c, c′ ).
                                                   ​                         ​                  ​                               ​




            Also
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                              399/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                     Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                                                   B(c, c) = 2ΠX (c).                      ​       ​




            Define the normal-ordered charge group
                                                                  ΓX = Γform
                                                                     ​




                                                                        X    ⊕B Γgram .​               ​               ​       ​




            As a set,
                                                                  ΓX = Γform
                                                                        X​   ⊕ Γgram .     ​                   ​




            Write elements
                                                                                 c = (c, T ).

            Define multiplication
                                          (c, T ) ⋆ (c′ , T ′ ) = (c + c′ , T + T ′ + B(c, c′ )).                                                  ​




            The identity is
                                                                                   (0, 0).

            The inverse is
                                                      (c, T )−1 = (−c, −T + B(c, c)).                                                          ​




            Associativity is the equality
                                     B(c, c′ ) + B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c′ , c′′ ) + B(c, c′ + c′′ ),

            which follows from bilinearity of B . Since B is symmetric, ΓX is abelian.                                                 ​




            Define
                                        ΠX : ΓX → Γgram ,
                                              ​        ​                     ​        ΠX (c, T ) = ΠX (c) − T .
                                                                                                   ​                               ​                   ​




            Then
                                                           ΠX (c ⋆ c′ ) = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ).
                                                              ​                                ​                           ​               ​




            Proof:

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                                 400/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                                               Attack Whitepaper Analysis

                                 ΠX ((c, T ) ⋆ (c , T )) = ΠX (c + c′ ) − T − T ′ − B(c, c′ )
                                       ​
                                                              ′   ′
                                                                                                            ​




                                                         = ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ) − T − T ′
                                                                              ​
                                                                                                            ​                       ​


                                                                                                                                                        ​




                                                                                      = ΠX (c, T ) + ΠX (c′ , T ′ ).
                                                                                                            ​                               ​




            This is the fundamental normal-ordering construction.

            2. Raw/split/cochain firewall
            The manuscript must distinguish three maps.
            2.1 Raw inclusion
                                                  i0 : Γform
                                                      ​




                                                        X    → ΓX ,   ​                             ​                 i0 (c) = (c, 0).
                                                                                                                            ​                   ​




            Then
                                                                  ΠX (i0 (c)) = ΠX (c).
                                                                                  ​         ​                                   ​




            But i0 is not a homomorphism:
                        ​




                                           i0 (c) ⋆ i0 (c′ ) = (c + c′ , B(c, c′ )) =
                                                          ​
                                                                                     (c + c′ , 0).

            This is the raw Hall lift.
            2.2 Zero-Gram splitting
            Define
                                                                  s0 (c) = (c, ΠX (c)).
                                                                          ​                                             ​               ​




            Then
                                                                          ΠX (s0 (c)) = 0.
                                                                                        ​               ​




            Moreover,
            s0 (c) ⋆ s0 (c′ ) = (c + c′ , ΠX (c) + ΠX (c′ ) + B(c, c′ )) = (c + c′ , ΠX (c + c′ )) = s0 (c + c′
               ​            ​                                 ​                                 ​                                                   ​       ​




            So s0 is a group homomorphism.
                    ​




            2.3 Linear target lift

                    sL (γ) ⋆ sL (η) = (Lγ + Lη, ΠX (Lγ) + ΠX (Lη) + B(Lγ, Lη) − γ − η ) .

            2.4 Cochain language

                                                                       2

                                                                             X ; Γgram )      ​                      ​                           ​




            for the additive group with trivial action.
            The cocycle identity is
                                     B(c, c′ ) + B(c + c′ , c′′ ) = B(c′ , c′′ ) + B(c, c′ + c′′ ).

            as a set-theoretic quadratic cochain:

            3. Raw descent no-go
            Let
                                                                      P = ⨁ Pc                                                   ​       ​

            Raw ΠX -pushforward would require
                            ​

            for every nonzero bracket, equivalently
                                                                       B(c, c′ ) = 0.

            But in the Gritsenko–Nikulin BKM algebra, for distinct real simple roots ei , ej ,                                                           ​       ​

                                                                                 ​  0,                  ​

                                                                      (ad ei )3 ej = 0.      ​                       ​




            If ci , cj are raw lifts, the first bracket forces
                    ​   ​




                                                                      B(ci , cj ) = 0.
                                                                                     ​                       ​

                                               ​       ​       ​                ​       ​        0.              ​       ​               ​   ​




            Therefore raw Gram descent cannot realize the full BKM bracket. This agrees with the
            manuscript’s current no-go/normal-ordering diagnosis. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…

            4. Finite active windows

            18. Universal property
            Define a category
                                                                                              DIHallΓ,E             ​       ​




            whose objects are filtered systems
                                                        H = {HR , PR , μ, Δ, OrR , HybR , CR , DR }
                                                                     ​                ​                         ​                           ​           ​   ​




            satisfying:
             1. PR ⊂ Prim(HR );
             2. HR is PBW-generated by PR ;
                        ​                                    ​




             3. degrees lie in ΓX ;
                                ​                                             ​




             4. multiplication respects ⋆;
                                                         ​




             5. ΠX gives the Gram grading;
             6. orientation is represented by square-root gerbes;
                                    ​




             7. CR is the source chiral bar coalgebra of the hybrid source;
             8. DR is first-order with scalar square on each primitive mode.
                            ​




                                ​




            A morphism preserves all these structures.
            Then UDIΔ ,E,L is the free object generated by the lifted BKM primitive algebra:
                                        5   ​
                                                ​




                                                    HomDIHall (UDI
                                                                Δ5 ,E,L , H) ≅ HomLieΓ (n+,L , Prim(H)).
                                                                 ​




                                                                         ​
                                                                                  ​


                                                                                                                                ​
                                                                                                                                    ​               ​           ​




            This is the Etingof–Kac-level universal property.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                                                                          422/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                                             Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            The proof is PBW/universal enveloping:
                                      HomHopf (U (n+,L ), H) ≅ HomLie (n+,L , Prim(H)).
                                                         ​        ​                                                                         ​               ​




            The orientation, hybrid, coalgebra, and Dirac structures are functorially induced from
            the primitive map.

                                                             Pf(UDI

                                                                      PR,γ = gΔ5 ,γ ,​


                                                                                                                            ​
                                                                                                                                        ​




            so
                                          sdim PR,(n,l,m) = sdim gΔ5 ,(n,l,m) = f (nm, l).
                                                              ​



                                                                                                                        ​
                                                                                                                                                    ​




            Thus
                                          Pf R = 64q 1/2 r1/2 s1/2 ∏ (1 − q n rl sm )f (nm,l) .
                                                 ​                                                                  ​




                                                                                             γ∈AR               ​




            Taking the cusp-completed limit over R,
                                            Pf = 64q 1/2 r1/2 s1/2 ∏(1 − q n rl sm )f (nm,l) .              ​




                                                                                             Γeff   ​




            By the Borcherds–Gritsenko–Nikulin product identity,
                                            64q 1/2 r1/2 s1/2 ∏(1 − q n rl sm )f (nm,l) = Δ5 .

                                                                      Γeff   ​

            20. Geometric realization theorem
            Now the compact K3 × E theorem is precise.
            Let
                                                                     Hgeom
                                                                      X                   ​




            be a geometric Hall/factorization object built from compact K3 × E branes, if
            constructed.
            A Dirac–Igusa realization functor is a compatible system
                                                             RX,R : Hgeom
                                                                 ​
                                                                            DI
                                                                     X,R → UR         ​                                   ​   ​




           such that:
            1. it preserves ΓX -degree;
            2. it sends geometric extension correspondences to μR,S ;
                                            ​




            3. it sends geometric coproduct correspondences to ΔR ;
                                                                                                                                      ​




            4. it identifies geometric primitives with PR ;
                                                                                                                                  ​




            5. it maps geometric orientation gerbes to OrR ;
                                                                              ​




            6. it sends local/wrapped geometric sectors to HybR ;
                                                                                                  ​




            7. it sends geometric source coalgebras to CR ;
                                                                                                                      ​




            8. it intertwines geometric Dirac blocks with DR ;
                                                                                              ​




            9. it preserves Pfaffian lines and cusp normalization;
                                                                                                      ​




           10. it is compatible in R.
           Then
                                                                Pf geom
                                                                   X    = Δ5 .
                                                                         ​                                ​   ​




            This is now a realization theorem, not a missing-object certificate.

            24. Final position
            At the standard you are invoking, one cannot honestly claim that the compact K3 × E
            derived Hall source has been geometrically constructed merely from the determinant,
            OP scalar square, or GN denominator algebra. The manuscript already says those
            implications do not exist. raeez.lorgat.automorphic-correc…
            What can be constructed rigorously, now, is the missing algebraic finite-source object:
                                                                    UDI

            cochains, orientation gerbes, hybrid colored operations, source coalgebras, Dirac
            blocks, and Pfaffians.

            Thought for 4m 31s
            According to a document from April 29, 2026, the latest WIP has made the right high-
            level correction: it now explicitly constructs only the target-side algebraic reference
            tower and states that the compact K3 × E Hall/factorization source remains a
            geometric realization problem. In particular, the manuscript says the target tower proves
            no compact K3 × E Hall source, no geometric Pfaffian line, no orientation monodromy,
            no source coalgebra, and no primitive recognition theorem; it formulates the missing
            compact object as finite geometric Hall–Dirac source data plus realization functors into
            the algebraic target tower. main
            That is a major improvement. It also means the next step is now sharply defined: do not
            try to call the target tower the source. Instead, add a new universal algebraic source
            object with an explicit universal property, then state the compact K3 × E theorem as a
            geometric realization of that source.
            Below is the construction-level patch I would insert.

            9. Universal property

            The WIP contains a proof sketch saying that, after the Borel class is killed, the stabilizer

            Problems:
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                                  455/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                   Attack Whitepaper Analysis

             1. A self-Ext complex is graded. Determinant means Berezinian of a complex, not
                determinant of a single vector space.
             2. “Conjugation has determinant 1” is true in simple finite-dimensional End(V )-type
                situations, but not automatically for a derived reduced self-Ext complex with trace
                removal, semiregularity quotient, E -reduction, and stabilizer action.
             3. The stabilizer action is not necessarily purely inner on every summand after
                quotienting and reduction.
             4. The square root can still have a nontrivial character even if the determinant line is
                equivariantly trivial.
             5. The claim that trace and semiregularity summands are trivial representations is a
                geometric assertion, not a formal consequence. It needs a lemma with the actual
                representation on those summands.
            This passage should be downgraded.

            24. The paper’s strongest possible honest thesis
30/04/2026, 01:14                                                         Attack Whitepaper Analysis




            Here is the thesis that survives brutal attack:
            \boxed{ \textit{ The Igusa cusp form }\Delta_5\textit{ is the Borcherds determinant of the
            K3 half-index and the denominator of the Gritsenko–Nikulin BKM algebra. The formal
            Mukai Gram map on }K3\times E\textit{ is quadratic, so any compact Hall realization
            must use a normal-ordered charge extension before Gram descent. Raw Gram descent
            cannot realize the BKM real-root brackets. The OP/DT scalar branch is the orientation-
            forgetful square. The missing object is a compact, oriented, hybrid local/wrapped
            Hall/factorization source whose protected primitives realize }\mathfrak
            g_{\Delta_5}\textit{ and whose Pfaffian line realizes }\Delta_5. } }
            That is strong. It is honest. It advances the architecture without pretending to have
            solved the compact geometry.

            28. The final “Beilinson filter”
            Apply this filter to every paragraph.
            Question 1
            Does this paragraph define an object with underlying data and maps?
            If no, it is not a construction.
            Question 2
            Does this paragraph prove a property of an already-defined object?
            If no, it is not a theorem.
            Question 3
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa55c-63c4-83ea-b3fc-fc3666b5910f                                         469/496
30/04/2026, 01:14                                            Attack Whitepaper Analysis

            Does this paragraph import a theorem?
            If yes, mark it imported and stop pretending it is a contribution.
            Question 4
            Does this paragraph assert equality of compact source and target?
            If yes, demand an actual source map.
            Question 5
            Does this paragraph infer geometry from an automorphic product?
            If yes, delete or downgrade.
            Question 6
            Does this paragraph infer orientation from a scalar square?
            If yes, delete.
            Question 7
            Does this paragraph call a target algebra a source?
            If yes, delete.

            29. The “dismiss false ideas” list
           Dismiss these completely:
            1. The determinant constructs a Hilbert space.
            2. The determinant constructs a vertex algebra.
            3. The determinant constructs a Hall algebra.
            4. The determinant constructs a Dirac operator.
            5. The determinant constructs an orientation.
            6. The scalar square chooses a square root.
            7. The OP sign is a Hall orientation sign.
            8. The Maass character is the Hall orientation character.
            9. The automorphic divisor order computes the Hall normal self-Ext rank.
           10. The BKM target algebra is the compact primitive algebra.
           11. PBW target windows are finite source data.

           12. Target bar–cobar is source coalgebra.
           13. A formal current envelope is a compact chiral source.
           14. A synthetic hybrid PROP is a geometric wrapped correspondence object.
           15. A square-root gerbe slogan is an orientation construction.
           16. Lattice normal-ordering is chain-level normal-ordering.
           17. K0-superdimension data gives parity spaces.
           18. Signed multiplicities give bracket constants.
